$parserComment: plan-24 Stage 7 cap lift. `maxExpressionDepth` is the Pratt walker's expression-NESTING cap -- the threshold of the positioned, fail-loud `P_ExpressionTooDeep` backstop -- now 100% config-driven: the loader reads this key into `ParserConfig::maxExpressionDepth` via `parserConfigFor` (src/analysis/compilation_unit/compilation_unit.cpp); the C++ field default (256) is only the fallback when this key is absent. RAISED 256 -> 1024 (4x). WHY 1024 and not higher: plan-24 flattened the parser's operator/ternary/prefix/infix descent and ALL of cst_to_hir/hir_to_mir/semantic/const-eval onto explicit work-stacks (O(1) host-stack per nesting level), so operator/ternary chains are now bounded ONLY by this cap. The ONE residual recursion is the parser's paren/postfix-body arm (the deferred Stage 5b), which still costs one host frame per nested '(' on the 64 MiB deep-recursion worker stack (core/substrate/large_stack_call `kDeepRecursionStackBytes`). The cap is a SAFETY BACKSTOP: it must trip BEFORE that arm overflows on the WORST supported build, otherwise a deep paren nest would crash (rc 127) instead of failing loud -- so the cap must sit BELOW the tightest crash ceiling, not just near it. MEASURED native-Windows paren-crash ceilings (deepest '(' nest that still compiled on the 64 MiB worker): MSVC Debug ~3000-3200, MSVC Release ~16000-16500, MinGW Release ~20000-24000. 1024 leaves ~3x headroom below the tightest (MSVC Debug) ceiling -- covering Debug-build frame bloat plus cross-platform (Linux/macOS) frame variance -- while being 4x the old 256 and far above any realistic machine-generated C nesting. A nest beyond 1024 still FAILS LOUD with the positioned diagnostic + recovery; it never crashes. toy/tsql omit the `parser` block, so they keep the 256 fallback.
$preprocessComment: FC13: opts c-subset into the config-SELECTED C preprocessor pass (src/analysis/preprocess/). `enabled:true` runs the pass between tokenize and parse: it builds ONE synthesized buffer by recursively splicing quote-#include"h" headers' text, tokenizes once, then runs the object-like macro pass (#define/#undef, expansion + rescan + blue-paint self-ref guard, directive removal). The TOKEN-name fields (directiveIntroToken/quoteIncludeToken/angleIncludeToken) are validated against the interned token kinds; the directive-keyword strings (define/undef/include) are matched by lexeme TEXT against the token after `#` (define/undef are NOT grammar keywords -- they lex as Identifier). Angle #include <h> is LEFT in place for the existing post-parse FF11 import resolver (so angleIncludeToken lets the PP RECOGNISE and pass it through). toy/tsql-subset declare NO preprocess block -> their pass is a strict identity (token stream in == out).

preprocess.$elifdefComment: C23 (D-PP-ELIFDEF-ELIFNDEF; C 6.10.1): the `#elifdef`/`#elifndef` directive WORDS. `#elifdef X` == `#elif defined(X)`, `#elifndef X` == `#elif !defined(X)` (C 6.10.1p5) -- routed through the SAME #elif conditional-group state machine, but evaluating the operand with the DIRECT #ifdef-style definedness lookup (the operand is a bare macro name, NOT run through the #if expression evaluator). Matched by lexeme TEXT (they lex as plain Identifier, like elif/else/endif). OPTIONAL: a config that omits them (or strips them) makes the directive fall through to the unsupported-directive fail-loud -- never a silent branch skip.
preprocess.$variadicCommaElisionComment: FC15 paste residuals (D-PP-VARIADIC-GNU-COMMA-ELISION): opt into the GNU `,##__VA_ARGS__` extension. `sep ## __VA_ARGS__` with an EMPTY variadic part drops the preceding separator (`LOG("x")` -> `f("x")`); non-empty keeps it. Without this flag, standard C placemarker behavior leaves the separator. The separator/__VA_ARGS__ are matched by the config KIND/name above, never hardcoded.
preprocess.$predefinedMacrosComment: FC15b (C 6.10.8): the predefined macros. Each entry names the macro IDENTIFIER + a materialization `kind`; the engine keys EVERY behavior off `kind`, never the name. `line`/`file` are OFFSET-derived (resolved through the line-map against the INVOCATION offset -> the invocation line / the presumed file name); `date`/`time` are computed once at preprocess construction (`"Mmm dd yyyy"` / `"hh:mm:ss"`); `constant` carries a static `value` spelling verbatim. `#define`/`#undef` of any of these is a constraint violation (C 6.10.8.1) -> fail loud. __STDC_VERSION__ = 202311L (C23). VLA support LANDED (D-CSUBSET-VLA C1b, 2026-07-13): `int a[n]` now RUNS (dynamic-stack `sub sp,<size>` + a conditional frame pointer, agnostic x86_64/arm64 x ELF/PE/Mach-O), so __STDC_NO_VLA__ is REMOVED from this table -- a VLA-SUPPORTING implementation must NOT define it (C11 6.10.8.3 / C23 6.10.9.3; the mirror of the fail-loud-era decision that defined it). __STDC_NO_THREADS__ (FC17.9(a) D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-TRAMPOLINES): REMOVED ENTIRELY. C11 6.10.8.3 / C23 6.10.9.3 make the macro the conforming announcement that <threads.h> is NOT provided; <threads.h> is now COMPLETE on ALL legs — thrd_create/call_once/thrd_join land on elf (libc.so.6 FFI), pe64 (kernel32 synth: CreateThread/InitOnceExecuteOnce/WaitForSingleObject) AND macho (libSystem pthread synth: pthread_create DIRECT-PASS / pthread_once DIRECT / pthread_join + truncate; the macho trampolines closed the last carve-out) — so a conforming impl must NOT define the macro on ANY target, and the row is deleted (was momentarily macho-only after Cycle 2 while macho lacked thrd_create). c95 (Windows-host sub-arc): the OPTIONAL `availableObjectFormats` per-entry filter (mirrors the shipped-lib descriptor field; EMPTY = every format, a non-empty set of object-format NAMES restricts the macro to those formats — validated against objectFormatKindFromName at load, an unknown name fails loud) lets the Windows-selection macros be predefined for the `pe` target ONLY: `_WIN32`/`_WIN64` (value 1) make sqlite's amalgamation select os_win.c (SQLITE_OS_WIN); `__stdcall`/`__cdecl`/`__fastcall`/`WINAPI` ERASE to nothing (empty value) since PE64 has a single MS-x64 calling convention and imports are un-decorated (a `__stdcall`-qualified declarator then parses on pe, stays an ordinary identifier off-pe). None leak onto elf/macho. The 7 core C 6.10.8 macros carry NO availableObjectFormats filter (available everywhere); the formerly-CONDITIONAL __STDC_NO_THREADS__ (C11 6.10.8.3 / C23 6.10.9.3) is now DELETED — threads.h is complete on all legs (see above).
preprocess.$fc15cComment: FC15c. `#pragma` (C 6.10.6): consumed-and-DROPPED (C 6.10.6p2 licenses ignoring an unrecognized pragma; DSS recognizes none). `__has_include` (C23 6.10.1p4): tests header existence with the SAME resolution `#include` uses -- the angle delimiters are matched by TOKEN KIND (`hasIncludeAngleOpenToken`=LtOp / `hasIncludeAngleCloseToken`=GtOp), never the `<`/`>` bytes (agnosticism); the angle form maps `<stem>.json` on the system path (DSS ships JSON descriptors, e.g. `stdio.json`) so `__has_include(<stdio.h>)` agrees with `#include <stdio.h>`. `__has_c_attribute` (C23 6.10.1p4): reports the version int of a known standard attribute or 0; the lookup ignores leading/trailing `__` (so `__deprecated__` matches `deprecated`). The C23 standard attributes are 202311.
preprocess.$fc179EmbedComment: FC17.9(h) C23 `#embed` (6.10.4) + `__has_embed` (6.10.1). `#embed "r"` resolves the QUOTED binary resource with the SAME search a quote-`#include` uses (self-dir first, then include dirs) and expands to the resource bytes as a comma-separated list of decimal `int` constants (0..255), spliced through the SAME product-token mechanism as `#`/`##` -- so the tokens are ordinary integer literals + commas the parser accepts in a brace initializer. `__has_embed` reports the C23 trichotomy NOT_FOUND(0)/FOUND(1)/EMPTY(2) (== the `__STDC_EMBED_*` macros above). CYCLE-1 SUBSET: the standard parameters (limit/prefix/suffix/if_empty + vendor) and the ANGLE form `<r>` and a macro-expanded argument are DEFERRED loud (D-PP-EMBED-{PARAMS,ANGLE,MACRO-ARG}); a resource above a ~16 MiB splice budget fails loud, never OOMs (the streaming boundary). `__has_embed` with any parameter clause answers NOT_FOUND(0) per C23 (the standard's own unsupported-signal, not an error). The angle delimiters are the SAME token KINDS as `__has_include` (hasIncludeAngleOpenToken/CloseToken), matched by KIND never the `<`/`>` bytes; the operator words are matched by TEXT, never a hard-coded spelling.

directive.$comment: FC13: the line-scoped C-directive mode. `#` pushes it (popAtNewline -> auto-pops at the next newline so a malformed directive never leaks state into the following line). It is an INLINE override table that lists ONLY `include`; the tokenizer falls back to the GLOBAL table for every other lexeme, so `define`/`undef`, `"..."`, identifiers AND operators all lex normally. CRUCIALLY there is NO `<`-override here: a `<` inside a NON-include directive (e.g. `#define X 1<<2`) falls back to the global `<<`/`<` operators -- no longer catastrophically mis-lexed. The `include` override re-emits IncludeKeyword AND arms the header context (replaceMode -> header-context) so the FOLLOWING `<h>` opens a header path. Scoping the mode-switch to THIS mode (rather than to the global `include` keyword) means a bare `include` identifier at main depth never triggers a replaceMode (which would abort at main-mode depth) -- it just lexes as IncludeKeyword with no side-effect.

header-context.$comment: FC13: armed by the `include` keyword (replaceMode). An INLINE override table lists ONLY `<`; the tokenizer falls back to the GLOBAL table for every other lexeme, so `"h"` still opens the quote-string body and identifiers lex normally. The ONE override is `<` -> HeaderStart (replaceMode -> header-body, stringStyle.endsAt `>`). The override-first longest-match rule means `<` wins at length 1 and the global `<<`/`<=` are deliberately suppressed inside an include header context -- correct, since `#include <<x>>` is not valid C. popAtNewline guards a malformed `#include` with no header.

$fc2CastExprComment: FC2 Part A: C-style cast `(type)expr` (castExpr/castTypeRef/castTypeBase/castOperand shapes). castExpr shares the `( ... )` opener with compoundLiteralExpr/parenExpr inside the speculative operand alt; the probe disambiguates structurally (a brace-init follows the compound literal's `)`, an expression follows the cast's). `commitRequiresTypeName` names the type-position child rule: a structurally-successful probe COMMITS only per the parser's GENERIC type-name triage — (1) a type that is NOT a lone Identifier (keyword/struct-tag/star/const present) always commits, those forms cannot be expressions; (2) a lone Identifier the binder sketch knows as a TYPE commits; (3) one it knows as a VALUE rolls back to the value reading (shadowing-aware); (4) an UNKNOWN lone Identifier commits iff the token after the type's closer is NOT an infix/postfix/ternary operator per the operator table (the value reading could not continue), else rolls back AND records an AmbiguousTypeNameCandidate for the compilation-unit oracle's one-shot reparse (cross-file typedefs). castTypeRef duplicates typeRefAllowingStruct PLUS a bare-Identifier base (typedef names in cast position). NOTE (FC7 D-FC7-NESTED-STRUCT-FIELD): typeRefAllowingStruct ITSELF now also carries the bare-Identifier alt — but it is consumed ONLY by structField/unionField, and a struct/union body has NO expression-statement production, so the C decl-vs-expr ambiguity (`T x;` vs `a * b;`) cannot arise there (a bare name in field-type position is unambiguously a type; a non-type fails loud at semantic scope-lookup). varDecl/param do NOT use typeRefAllowingStruct — they use declHead/declHeadForParam with the binder-sketch `commitRequiresTypeName` commit — so the statement/param decl-vs-expr triage is untouched by that alt. castOperand is a second expr-entry rule re-entering the Pratt walker at PREFIX precedence (90): the cast operand binds prefix + postfix operators but NOT binary/ternary ones, giving C-correct `(int)a + b` == ((int)a)+b, `(int)x[2]` == (int)(x[2]), `-(int)x`, and `(int)(long)x` chains. Same wrapper vocabulary as `expression` (wrapper rule names are per-schema; the loader dedups).
$ff11AngleIncludeComment: FF11 angle-include lexing (FC13 mode structure). `#` pushes the line-scoped `directive` mode (popAtNewline:true -> auto-pops at the next newline, so a malformed `#include` -- or any directive -- never leaks state into the following line; C directives are line-oriented). CRUCIALLY `directive` no longer overrides `<`: the ONLY override there is `include` (-> IncludeKeyword + replaceMode to `header-context`), so a `<` in a NON-include directive (e.g. `#define X 1<<2`) falls back to the global `<<`/`<` operators -- no longer mis-lexed as a header opener (the headline FC13 lexer fix). The `<`-OPENS-a-header-path override moved DOWNSTREAM to `header-context` (armed by `include`): there `<` -> HeaderStart (replaceMode -> header-body, stringStyle.endsAt `>`), while `"h"` still opens the quote-string body. The override-first longest-match rule (language-config-spec section 9.6) makes `<` win at length 1 inside `header-context` so `<<`/`<=` are suppressed THERE only -- correct, since `#include <<x>>` is not valid C. The `header-body` mode mirrors the `string` body: a coalesced `HeaderPath` captures the raw path bytes between `<` and the closing `>` (which pops back to main); escapeKind:none. The quote form keeps the global `"`->StringStart->string body unchanged (both `#include <h>` and `#include "h"` work). The resolver reads the raw path slice (a filename, not a decoded string) for both forms, and picks the search path by which token kind it finds under the directive (imports.pathToken=StringStart -> self-dir+includeDirs; imports.systemPathToken=HeaderStart -> shippedLibDirs).

//.$endsAtExclusiveComment: c22 (D-PP-LINE-COMMENT-BEFORE-DIRECTIVE): a `//` comment ends AT the newline but EXCLUDES it — the newline must survive as its own Newline token, else a directive on the next line after `code // comment` loses its line boundary and the preprocessor fails to recognize it (firstOnLine sees the comment-consumed newline as a body char, not a boundary).

L\".$comment: C11/C23 6.4.5 wide string `L"…"`. A multi-char lexeme so the tokenizer's longestMatchInMode beats the bare `L` identifier run; pushes the SAME shared `string` mode as `"` (one coalesced StringLiteral body). Its element core (wchar_t) is FORMAT-keyed in semantic (D-FFI-STDDEF-WCHAR-PE-WIDTH), so hirLowering.stringLiteralPrefixes carries only a placeholder for it.

u\".$comment: C11/C23 6.4.5 char16_t string `u"…"` → UTF-16 code units (U16). Same shared `string` mode as `"`; longestMatchInMode makes `u"` beat the `u` id-run.

U\".$comment: C11/C23 6.4.5 char32_t string `U"…"` → UTF-32 code units (U32). Same shared `string` mode as `"`.

u8\".$comment: C11/C23 6.4.5 UTF-8 string `u8"…"` → UTF-8 bytes (char8_t=Char-width). longestMatchInMode makes `u8"` (3 bytes) beat both the `u8` id-run and the `u"` opener. Same shared `string` mode as `"`.

L'.$comment: C11/C23 6.4.4.4 wide char constant `L'x'` (wchar_t). A multi-char lexeme so the tokenizer's longestMatchInMode beats the bare `L` identifier run; pushes the SAME shared `charBody` mode as `'` (one coalesced CharLiteral body). Its element core (wchar_t) is FORMAT-keyed in semantic (D-FFI-STDDEF-WCHAR-PE-WIDTH), mirroring the wide-STRING `L"` opener.

u'.$comment: C11/C23 6.4.4.4 char16_t constant `u'x'` → one UTF-16 code unit (U16). Same shared `charBody` mode as `'`; longestMatchInMode makes `u'` beat the `u` id-run.

U'.$comment: C11/C23 6.4.4.4 char32_t constant `U'x'` → one UTF-32 code unit (U32). Same shared `charBody` mode as `'`.

u8'.$comment: C23 6.4.4.4 char8_t constant `u8'x'` → one UTF-8 code unit (U8, must be ≤ U+007F). longestMatchInMode makes `u8'` (3 bytes) beat both the `u8` id-run and the `u'` opener. Same shared `charBody` mode as `'`.

topLevel.$comment: NON-speculative: each alt has a disjoint FIRST. struct/union/enum heads route to topLevelDecl (via topLevelHead's body/ref specifier forms) — the bare top-level structDecl/unionDecl/enumDecl rules were folded into topLevelDecl (optional declarator) so a `struct` lead has ONE owner here and only topLevelHead's single-level speculation runs (the two-level topLevel-speculation path was unsound — D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION). Those three now-unreferenced rules (+ their semantics/hirKind/scopes rows) were DELETED in the closing cycle (no dead config); the shared structField/unionField/enumerator helpers stay (the *SpecifierBody forms consume them). c81: `emptyStmt` (a lone `;`) is admitted as an external declaration — sqlite shell.c's `SQLITE_EXTENSION_INIT1;` expands EMPTY in the static-shell build, leaving a stray file-scope semicolon (x2, base64.c/base85.c sections) that gcc/clang accept (-pedantic warns; C 6.9 formally disallows it, but accepting it is the universal implementation posture). REUSES the statement-position `emptyStmt` rule exactly as c30 reuses `typedefDecl` in both positions: FIRST = {EndStatement}, disjoint from every other alt -> unique-production direct descent, still non-speculative; its existing `hirKind: Skip` row lowers it to nothing in BOTH positions (the includeDirective->Skip top-level precedent), so no semantics/scopes row is needed.

structMemberDeclSpecifiers.$comment: C11/C23 6.7.5 (D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS): the OPTIONAL leading alignment-specifier prefix of a struct/union member — one or more `alignasSpec` (`alignas(16) int a;`; C permits several, the strictest wins). A SEQUENCE (first + `{repeat}`) so its tree node is `structMemberDeclSpecifiers`, matched by the DeclarationRule `specifierPrefix` on structField/unionField and STRIPPED by decl_prefix_strip.hpp before positional-child counting — so structField's `head:0, declaratorList:1` indices stay stable whether or not a member carries alignas. FIRST = {AlignasKeyword, AlignasLegacyKeyword}, disjoint from `typeRefAllowingStruct`'s FIRST, so the enclosing `{optional}` is predictively decidable (non-speculative).

structField.$comment: c23 D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR: a struct/union field is a type-specifier HEAD + an OPTIONAL `structMemberDeclaratorList` (a comma-separated list of per-slot declarators sharing the head base type, e.g. `int *a, b[4], *c;` — C 6.7.2.1) + `EndStatement`. SUPERSEDES c10's single `{optional declarator}{optional bitfieldDeclSuffix}`: each slot (`structMemberDeclarator`) now holds its OWN `declarator` AND its OWN `bitfieldDeclSuffix` so `int a:3, b:5;` resolves each width per-slot. Routing the member through the SAME `declarator` every other position uses (params/locals/globals) makes a function-pointer member (`int (*f)(int);`) parse + type-derive identically; the array suffix (`int arr[3];`) lives inside the declarator's OWN directDeclarator suffix chain. The LIST is OPTIONAL to admit the `int ;` declares-nothing form; a single-slot list with absent declarator + present suffix is the anonymous bit-field (`int : 3;` / zero-width `int : 0;`); the over-admitted nameless non-bitfield forms FAIL LOUD at semantic composition (anonymous member must be a bit-field). C11/C23 6.7.5 (D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS): an OPTIONAL leading `structMemberDeclSpecifiers` (`alignas(N) …`) precedes the head — declared as the `specifierPrefix` (below) so it is stripped before the head:0/declaratorList:1 count.

bitfieldDeclSuffix.$comment: FC8 D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD: the `: width` suffix; the width is a constant-expression evaluated + range-checked against the field's integer type at semantic.

structMemberDeclarator.$comment: c23 D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR: ONE slot of a struct/union member list — an OPTIONAL `declarator` (its OWN pointer/array/fn suffixes; the `*` binds PER-SLOT so `int *a, b;` is Ptr<int> then int) + an OPTIONAL `bitfieldDeclSuffix` (now INSIDE the slot so `int a:3, b:5;` resolves widths independently). The declarator is optional to admit an anonymous bit-field (`int : 3;` — absent declarator, present suffix). The engine's `memberDeclaratorRule` role descends to the inner `declarator` for name + type derivation.

structMemberDeclaratorList.$comment: c23 D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR: `structMemberDeclarator (, structMemberDeclarator)*` — mirrors `initDeclaratorList` for struct/union members. The engine's `memberListRule` role iterates the slot children (commas skipped).

structTypeRef.$comment: c25: the REF-ONLY struct specifier, kept for the param/cast positions (declHeadForParam, castTypeBase) where a struct type is a pure tag reference. The DECLARATION-head positions no longer use this pair-with-body form — they route through the UNIFIED `structSpec` (below), which folds body-vs-ref into ONE rule so it is the SOLE candidate for `struct` at those heads → the parser's unique-production direct descent parses the member body NON-speculatively (no probe, no token budget — the c25 fix for `struct sqlite3`).

structSpec.$comment: c25 D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER: the UNIFIED struct specifier (C 6.7.2.1 struct-or-union-specifier) — `struct {opt tag} {opt body}`. ONE rule for BOTH the type-DEFINITION form (`struct P { … }` — a `structBody` child is present) and the bare tag-REFERENCE form (`struct P` — absent). Replaces the former `structSpecifierBody | structTypeRef` SPECULATIVE pair in the 4 body-bearing declaration-head alts (topLevelCompositeSpec, typeSpecifierForDecl, typedefHead, typeBaseAllowingStruct). Because it is now the SOLE candidate keyed on StructKeyword in those alts, the parser's LL(k) unique-production DIRECT DESCENT (parser.cpp ~1804-1844) dispatches it NON-speculatively — the member list parses with NO speculation budget, so an arbitrarily large body (`struct sqlite3`) never trips the 4096-token probe cliff. The body-vs-ref split becomes an INTERNAL 1-token (`BlockOpen`) decision via the `{optional structBody}`, also non-speculative. The binder is DUAL-MODE on the SAME node (semantics: `definesWhenChild: structBody`): body present ⇒ the EXACT former `structSpecifierBody` define path (mint type, bind tag, open scope, compose fields from the `structBody` subtree); body absent ⇒ the EXACT former `structTypeRef` tag-reference path (isTagReference). FOLLOW-independent (the rejected D2): no prune of the ref candidate is needed because there IS no separate ref candidate here.

structBody.$comment: c25: the brace-delimited struct member list, factored OUT of `structSpec` so its PRESENCE is the define-vs-reference discriminator (the binder's `definesWhenChild`). NOT a scope rule itself: the fields bind into the scope opened by the enclosing `structSpec` (the analyzer's Pass-1.5 field collection walks that scope, not direct children, so the extra nesting level is transparent). Member shape identical to the former `structSpecifierBody` tail.

unionField.$comment: c23 D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR: a union field mirrors `structField` — a type-specifier HEAD + an OPTIONAL `structMemberDeclaratorList` (per-slot declarators sharing the head, e.g. `int *a, b;`) + `EndStatement`. The per-slot `structMemberDeclarator` holds its own declarator (function-pointer-member support: `int (*f)(int);`) AND its own bitfield suffix. The array suffix lives inside the declarator's directDeclarator suffix chain. C11/C23 6.7.5 (D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS): an OPTIONAL leading `structMemberDeclSpecifiers` (`alignas(N) …`) precedes the head — the shared member-specifier prefix (same rule + `specifierPrefix` strip as structField).

unionTypeRef.$comment: c25: the REF-ONLY union specifier, kept for the param/cast positions (declHeadForParam, castTypeBase). The declaration-head positions route through the UNIFIED `unionSpec` (below). Mirrors structTypeRef.

unionSpec.$comment: c25 D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER: the UNIFIED union specifier — `union {opt tag} {opt body}`. Mirrors `structSpec`: ONE rule for define (`union U { … }`) and reference (`union U`), the SOLE UnionKeyword candidate in the 4 body-bearing alts → non-speculative direct descent (no member-body probe budget). Dual-mode binder via `definesWhenChild: unionBody`.

unionBody.$comment: c25: the brace-delimited union member list, factored out of `unionSpec` as the define-vs-reference discriminator. NOT a scope rule (fields bind into the enclosing unionSpec's scope). Member shape identical to the former `unionSpecifierBody` tail.

enumTypeRef.$comment: c25: the REF-ONLY enum specifier, kept for the param/cast positions (declHeadForParam, castTypeBase). The declaration-head positions route through the UNIFIED `enumSpec` (below). Mirrors structTypeRef.

enumTypeSpecifier.$comment: C23 6.7.2.2 (D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE, FC17): the OPTIONAL `: <integer-type>` clause that fixes an enum's underlying type — `enum E : unsigned char { A }`. A SEQUENCE `[Colon, castTypeBase]` (castTypeBase is the qualifier-free/pointer-free base type-name — `unsigned char`, `int`, a typedef, NOT `int *`). It carries `commitRequiresTypeName` with `requireKnownType` polarity (MIRRORS `alignasTypeName`): the enum-underlying `:` collides with the pre-existing anonymous enum-typed struct bit-field `enum Color : 4;`, so the parser SPECULATIVELY tries this clause after the `:` and the binder triage commits ONLY on a real type (keyword/tag-led base → Rule 1; a KNOWN typedef → Rule 2); a bit-field WIDTH (an int-constant `4`, or a value/unknown identifier `enum Tag : SOME_WIDTH`) ROLLS BACK, leaving the `:` for the member-level bit-field grammar. SEMANTICS live in the semantic tier (DeclarationRule.enumUnderlyingType): the resolved integer TypeKind becomes the enum's underlying scalar (scalars[0]) and each enumerator's value is range-checked against it (fail-loud S_InvalidEnumUnderlyingType on a non-integer, S_EnumeratorValueOutOfRange on overflow).

enumSpec.$comment: c25 D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER: the UNIFIED enum specifier — `enum {opt tag} {opt underlying-type} {opt body}`. Mirrors `structSpec`: ONE rule for define (`enum E { A, B }`) and reference (`enum E`), the SOLE EnumKeyword candidate in the 4 body-bearing alts → non-speculative direct descent. Dual-mode binder via `definesWhenChild: enumBody`. The enumerator-list (with trailing comma) lives in `enumBody`. FC17 (D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE / D-PARSE-SPECULATIVE-OPTIONAL): the optional C23 `: <integer-type>` clause (`enumTypeSpecifier`) sits between the tag and the body as a SPECULATIVE optional so `enum E : unsigned char { A }` fixes the underlying type while the pre-existing anonymous enum-typed bit-field `enum Color : 4;` still parses (the clause is TRIED after the `:` and ROLLS BACK to the bit-field reading when a type doesn't follow — the whole point of the speculative-optional engine capability).

enumBody.$comment: c25: the brace-delimited enumerator list, factored out of `enumSpec` as the define-vs-reference discriminator. NOT a scope rule (enumerators bind into the enclosing enumSpec's scope, then lift to the enclosing namespace scope per fieldChildren.liftToEnclosingScope). Enumerator-list shape (optional list, trailing comma) identical to the former `enumSpecifierBody` tail.

topLevelDecl.$comment: C declaration = decl-specifiers init-declarator-list? `;` (or a function-definition block tail). The init-declarator-list is OPTIONAL: a bare `struct P { … };` / `enum E { … };` is a type-only declaration (head introduces the tag, no object declared) — the semantic tier mints the type from the head and emits a TypeDecl; a head that introduces NO tag with no declarator (`int ;`) is a C6.7p2 'declares nothing' constraint violation (fail-loud). A trailing declarator (`struct P { … } v;`) or a block tail (`struct Tri mkTri(void){…}`) takes the normal global/function path.

topLevelHead.$comment: Top-level head: a keyword type specifier (`typeSpecifierSeq`, e.g. `int`/`unsigned long`), a struct/union/enum composite specifier (`topLevelCompositeSpec` — the SPECULATIVE body-vs-ref alt), OR a lone Identifier (typedef name). The OUTER alt is NON-speculative: FIRST(typeSpecifierSeq)={type keywords}, FIRST(topLevelCompositeSpec)={struct,union,enum}, FIRST(Identifier)={ident} are pairwise disjoint — so a plain `int x;` routes DIRECTLY to typeSpecifierSeq (NO extra wrapper node, preserving the pre-existing tree shape) and only a struct/union/enum lead enters the single-level speculation that closes D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION. topLevel stays non-speculative: `struct` routes unambiguously to topLevelDecl.

topLevelCompositeSpec.$comment: c25 D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER: the struct/union/enum SPECIFIER at a top-level declaration head — now ONE unified rule per keyword (`structSpec`/`unionSpec`/`enumSpec`), each covering BOTH the body form (`struct P { … }`) AND the bare tag reference (`struct P`) via an internal `{optional body}`. The three candidates have pairwise-DISJOINT 1-token FIRSTs (StructKeyword/UnionKeyword/EnumKeyword), so the predictive prune leaves EXACTLY ONE survivor for any composite lead → the parser's unique-production DIRECT DESCENT (parser.cpp ~1804-1844) dispatches it NON-speculatively. The member body therefore parses with NO speculation budget — the c25 fix for `struct sqlite3`'s large body (the former body-vs-ref probe budget-capped at 4096 tokens). `speculative`/`lookahead` are retained but moot under the unique-survivor direct descent (kept so a future non-disjoint addition still has a probe budget). The enum body's trailing-comma enumerator-list parses cleanly (the schema compiler propagates nested-nullable FOLLOW via recomputeAltExpectedSets).

topLevelDeclTail.$comment: FIRST(block)={BlockOpen} vs {EndStatement} — disjoint. A block tail makes the FIRST init-declarator a function DEFINITION (semantic-tier checks enforce: exactly one declarator, named, fn-suffix-bearing, no initializer); an EndStatement tail makes every declarator a global variable.

singleDeclSpecifier.$comment: C11/C23 6.7.5 (D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS): `alignasSpec` (`_Alignas`/`alignas`) joins the top-level declaration-specifier alts. Its FIRST = {AlignasKeyword, AlignasLegacyKeyword} is DISJOINT from StaticKeyword / attrSpec's AttributeKeyword / stdAttr's BracketOpen, so the repeat stays predictively pruned (non-speculative). It is stripped from the positional-child count (specifierPrefix: declSpecifiers on topLevelDecl) and IGNORED by the linkage scan (linkageSpecifierIgnoredRules). FC16 (D-CSUBSET-NORETURN): the bare `_Noreturn` keyword (NoreturnKeyword) likewise joins the alts — FIRST {NoreturnKeyword} disjoint from all the above, so no speculation impact; it is stripped from the child count and IGNORED by the linkage scan (linkageSpecifierIgnoredKinds); the SEMANTIC noreturn scan reads it. The attribute forms (`[[noreturn]]`/`__attribute__((noreturn))`) ride the existing attrSpec/stdAttr alts. FC17 (D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR, C23 6.7.1): the `constexpr` storage-class specifier (ConstexprKeyword) joins the alts — FIRST {ConstexprKeyword} disjoint from all the above (a distinct exact-match keyword; `const` is a headQualifier, not a specifier, so no prefix collision), stripped from the child count like the others. At FILE scope it IS a linkage specifier: C23 6.2.2p3 gives a file-scope constexpr object INTERNAL linkage, so topLevelDecl's linkageSpecifiers maps the keyword text to {binding:local} (it is deliberately NOT in that row's linkageSpecifierIgnoredKinds). The SEMANTIC constexpr validation (Pass 2 validateConstexprDeclarator) reads it via semantics.constexpr.keywordToken.
singleDeclSpecifier.$threadLocalComment: TLS C1 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL, C11/C23 6.7.1): the `_Thread_local`/`thread_local` twin keywords (the _Alignof/alignof spelling precedent) join the alts — FIRST {ThreadLocalKeyword, ThreadLocalC23Keyword} disjoint from every other branch (distinct exact-match keywords), stripped from the child count like the others. The repeat admits both orders with `static` (`static thread_local` / `thread_local static`). NOT a linkage-changing specifier: the topLevelDecl row maps both spellings to {threadStorage:true} — a file-scope thread_local object keeps EXTERNAL linkage (6.2.2 untouched); Pass 1's specifier scan marks the symbol isThreadLocal and Pass 2's validateThreadLocalDeclarator enforces 6.7.1.

attrSpec.$comment: FC4 c1: the argument grew the parenthesized-string form `visibility("hidden")` — an Identifier optionally followed by `( string-literal )`. The inline sequences add NO wrapper nodes, so the bare `weak`/`static` tree shapes are byte-identical to pre-FC4. linkageFrom's composite-key lookup (`visibility:hidden`) pairs the identifier with the decoded string body.

stdAttr.$comment: C23 [[...]] attribute specifier (D14: parsed, semantically IGNORED — linkageSpecifierIgnoredRules skips the whole subtree in the linkage scan). Namespaced `[[gnu::attr]]` and a parenthesized string argument are accepted; richer argument forms are residue.

compositeAttr.$comment: FC16 (D-CSUBSET-PACKED): ONE composite type-attribute — a GNU `__attribute__((…))` (`attrSpec`) or a C23 `[[…]]` (`stdAttr`), REUSING the existing attribute rules. The trailing struct/union attribute list (`compositeAttrList`) is a repeat of this.

compositeAttrList.$comment: FC16 (D-CSUBSET-PACKED): the trailing composite-attribute list after a struct/union body — one or more `compositeAttr` (`struct S {…} __attribute__((packed))` / `[[gnu::packed]]`). Referenced as a TRAILING `{optional}` on structSpec/unionSpec (after `structBody`, so it never shifts the tag's positional index `name:1`). The semantic tier scans this node (semantics.packed.listRule) for a `packed` spelling. The after-KEYWORD position (`struct __attribute__((packed)) S`) is a SEPARATE deferred slot (D-CSUBSET-PACKED-AFTER-KEYWORD-POSITION) — it stays a parse error (fail-loud), never a silent drop.

declarator.$comment: alt of (pointer-led, direct-led): NON-nullable (at least one star or a direct), so `int = 5;` / `int;` stay parse errors; the pointer-led branch's OPTIONAL direct admits the abstract star-only form (`int *` in param position). The branch sequences are INLINE so pointerLayer/directDeclarator land as DIRECT children — the shape the engine's declarators roles describe.

directDeclarator.$comment: Base alt is INLINE + SPECULATIVE: fnSuffix vs parenDeclarator genuinely collide on `(` (C 6.7.6.3p11) — fnSuffix probes FIRST (declared order) under its requireKnownType guard; rollback yields the parenthesized-declarator reading. Identifier rides the token-leaf route (no probe); arrayStarSuffix/arrayDeclSuffix at base are the abstract `int [*]` / `int [3]` param forms. lookahead 64 = the operand-alt precedent (1024-token probe budget): a probe must swallow a whole parenthesized param list / nested declarator. VLA C4c (D-CSUBSET-VLA): the suffix repeat is SPECULATIVE and lists arrayStarSuffix (the fixed 3-token `[ * ]`) BEFORE arrayDeclSuffix so `int a[*]` (a prototype-form unspecified-size VLA parameter) probes arrayStarSuffix first and, on a real bound (`[N]`/`[*p]`), rolls back CLEANLY to arrayDeclSuffix — enabled by the schema-compiler propagating `speculative` onto an inline repeat-alt's loopEntry (grammar_schema_json.cpp repeat arm). fnSuffix still leads (declared order) since it is FIRST-disjoint (`(`).

fnSuffixTail.$comment: VLA C4c (§B 2026-07-14): the POST-BASE (suffix-position) function-suffix `( params )` — grammar-IDENTICAL to `fnSuffix` but WITHOUT the `commitRequiresTypeName` guard. Needed because the suffix repeat is now SPECULATIVE (to disambiguate arrayStarSuffix `[*]` vs arrayDeclSuffix): a guarded `fnSuffix` there would be PROBED even as a FIRST-unique sole survivor on `(` (the sole-survivor direct-descent skips guarded candidates so the binder triage runs), and its `requireKnownType` guard would spuriously ROLL BACK a valid function's `()` (empty params) / cross-file-typedef params — breaking `int f()`, casts, every function declarator. The guard is MEANINGLESS at the suffix position (there is no `parenDeclarator` competitor — that ambiguity is resolved once, at the BASE alt which KEEPS `fnSuffix`), so dropping it is C-correct. Semantically fnSuffixTail IS a function suffix: the `fnSuffixTailRule` config role makes `directDeclaredType` fold it → `FnSig` identically to `fnSuffix`, and the fn-declarator / definition-scope detection recognizes both.

abstractDirectDeclarator.$comment: c26 D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-DECLARATOR-TYPE-NAME: the abstract twin of `directDeclarator` for the CAST/sizeof/compound/va_arg type-name tail (C 6.7.7 direct-abstract-declarator). The BASE alt is deliberately ONLY `{fnSuffix, parenDeclarator}` — NOT the full `directDeclarator` base — for two reasons. (1) `Identifier` EXCLUDED: a bare-identifier base would make `(c * c)` (a pervasive multiplication) speculatively commit as a cast `(c *<abstract-decl c>)` — the type child gains >1 leaf so the commit triage's rule-1 'not a lone identifier' fires. (2) `arrayDeclSuffix` EXCLUDED at the BASE (the bare `[N]` form): `(b[0])` (an array-INDEX value expression) would likewise mis-commit as a type-name `b[0]` (base `b` + array suffix). C's direct-abstract-declarator allows a bare `[N]` base, but it collides with the index expression under speculative type-vs-value triage — so the BARE-array type-name (`(int[10])` / `sizeof(int[10])`) is the NAMED LIMITATION D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-ARRAY-TYPE-NAME (off the c26 fn-ptr frontier; the `(*)[N]` array-OF-pointer form still works — its base is `parenDeclarator`, the `[N]` rides the suffix repeat). The remaining base forms — `parenDeclarator` (`(*)`/`(*p)` — a nested NAME still parses and is rejected loud at the type-resolve), `fnSuffix` — REUSE the existing declarator sub-rules verbatim, so the SHARED semantic `directDeclaredType` folds it (`(*)(void)`→Ptr<Fn(void)->int>) identically to `directDeclarator`. The suffix repeat KEEPS `arrayDeclSuffix` so `(*)[N]` works. Speculative base (fnSuffix vs parenDeclarator collide on `(`, exactly as directDeclarator).

fnSuffix.$comment: The commit guard fires ONLY when fnSuffix is probed speculatively (the directDeclarator BASE position); the post-base suffix position dispatches non-speculatively on `(` and never consults it. requireKnownType = C 6.7.6.3p11: a lone unknown identifier between the parens rolls back to the parenthesized-declarator reading AND records the ambiguous-type-name candidate for the CU oracle's seeded reparse.

typeSpecifierForDecl.$comment: KEYWORD/composite-led type specifiers for declaration heads — deliberately NO Identifier arm (the typedef-name head is the ambiguous form, routed through identDeclHead/declOrExprStmt or the committed post-specifier/post-qualifier heads). SPECULATIVE (mirror typedefHead, D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION): admits the struct/union/enum BODY forms so a body-defined LOCAL (`struct P { int a; } v;` as a statement) parses; body forms probe FIRST, a ref with no `{` rolls back to the `*TypeRef` form. Single-level. D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF: `typeofSpecifier` (keyword-led, disjoint FIRST) is admitted here so a `typeof(...)`-headed declaration parses.

declHead.$comment: The COMMITTED any-form head: reached only after a leading storage-class specifier run (`register T x;`) — the statement is already known to be a declaration, so the Identifier (typedef-name) arm is unambiguous here.

kwDeclHead.$comment: The keyword-or-qualifier-led head (the non-speculative varDecl statement branch). Qualifier-led admits the Identifier (typedef) base — `const T x;` is committed by the `const` (no expression starts with a qualifier). Keyword-led is the plain `int x;` form. FIRSTs {Const,Volatile} vs type-keywords/struct/union/enum — disjoint.

identDeclHead.$comment: The AMBIGUOUS bare-typedef-name head (`T x;` vs the expression `T(x)`/`a*b`): lone Identifier + trailing qualifiers only. Leading qualifiers/specifiers route to kwDeclHead/varDecl instead (any of them commits a declaration).

typedefHead.$comment: SPECULATIVE: the struct/union/enum BODY forms collide with the ref forms on their lead keyword — body forms probe FIRST (declared order); `typedef struct P MyP;` rolls back to structTypeRef when no `{` follows. lookahead 256 (4096-token budget): a body probe must swallow the whole member list. D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF: `typeofSpecifier` (keyword-led, disjoint FIRST) admits `typedef typeof(x) T;`.

externDecl.$threadLocalComment: TLS C1 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): the former bare `ExternKeyword` child 0 is now the `externSpecifiers` HEAD WRAPPER (`extern` + an optional thread-storage specifier run) so `extern thread_local int e;` parses (C11 6.7.1p3 — extern IS one of the two specifiers thread_local may pair with; the cross-TU TLS declaration form). The wrapper occupies exactly the ONE child slot the keyword did AND is the row's `specifierPrefix`, so the shared first-child strip (decl_prefix_strip.hpp / the parser sketch's strip) renumbers the role children to [typeRef, Identifier, externTail] = name 1 / type 0 / kindByChild [2,0] REGARDLESS of specifier presence — no index shift, no new engine mechanism. FIRST(externDecl) stays {ExternKeyword}. The REVERSE order `thread_local extern` stays a loud parse error (topLevelDecl's head never matches `extern`), matching the `extern constexpr` posture.

externSpecifiers.$comment: TLS C1 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): `extern` plus an optional storage-specifier run — today only the thread-storage twins (`extern thread_local` / `extern _Thread_local`). FIRST(repeat) = {ThreadLocalKeyword, ThreadLocalC23Keyword}, DISJOINT from FIRST(typeRef) (type keywords / Identifier / typeof) → predictively pruned, non-speculative. As externDecl's specifierPrefix this subtree is scanned by linkageFrom: the row maps both thread-local spellings to {threadStorage:true} and ignores ExternKeyword by kind (the extern-ness is the RULE's identity — externDecl mints a non-defining declaration — not a linkage entry).

initValue.$comment: FC5: assignmentExpr (NOT expression) — the comma-gate. Covers ALL three initializer consumers (initDeclarator, structField, braceInitList initElement), so `int a = b, c;` stays 2 declarators (not `a = (b,c)`).

arrayDeclSuffix.$comment: VLA C4c (D-CSUBSET-VLA, C99 §6.7.6.2/6.7.6.3): the ONE array-declarator suffix `[ … ]`, SHARED across EVERY declarator-mode row (param, varDecl, structField, typedefDecl, forDecl) + the legacy externDecl. It admits the C99 array-PARAMETER decorations — a leading `static` and/or cv-qualifier run before the length BOUND (`int a[static 3]`, `int a[const n]`, `int a[const static 10]`). These are legal ONLY inside a function-parameter declarator; a NON-param use is a constraint violation caught SEMANTICALLY (the paramDecay-gated S_ArrayParamQualifierNonParameter), mirroring the typeSpecifierSeq → S_InvalidTypeSpecifierCombination discipline — the parser is deliberately permissive rather than forking a param-only sub-grammar. The qualifier run is INLINE keyword tokens (they land as DIRECT children, like typeSpecifierSeq's specifier run) and is FIRST-disjoint from the bound (no expression begins with static/const/volatile/restrict), so it is NON-speculative — a plain leading run before the unchanged `{optional expression}` bound. `int a[*p]` (a deref-sized VLA, LEGAL) keeps parsing `*p` as the bound EXPRESSION (unchanged). The bound's CST position is NO LONGER a fixed index (a leading qualifier shifts it) — every bound-locating site skips the decorations via the shared arraySuffixBoundNode helper keyed on DeclaratorConfig.arraySuffixModifierTokens. LANDED (D-CSUBSET-VLA-PARAM-STAR): the bare unspecified-size `*` form `int a[*]` (a prototype VLA-parameter marker) is admitted via the DISTINCT `arrayStarSuffix` = `[BracketOpen,StarOp,BracketClose]` rule (NOT this `arrayDeclSuffix`) + the speculative-repeat-alt schema-compiler engine fix (grammar_schema_json.cpp propagates `speculative` onto an inline repeat-alt's loopEntry, so the suffix repeat probes `arrayStarSuffix` then rolls back CLEANLY to this `arrayDeclSuffix` for a real bound). `int a[*p]` (a deref-sized VLA, LEGAL) still parses `*p` as THIS rule's bound EXPRESSION — the star-modifier is its own fixed-3-token rule, so this flat bound locator is UNTOUCHED. `StarOp` also stays in arraySuffixModifierTokens (the arraySuffixBoundNode scan skips a stray `*`).

arrayStarSuffix.$comment: VLA C4c (D-CSUBSET-VLA, C99 §6.7.6.2p4): the bare unspecified-size `[ * ]` array-declarator suffix — a prototype-form VLA-parameter marker. A FIXED 3-token sequence with its OWN CST node (distinct from arrayDeclSuffix), so the flat bound-locator / lengthChild / captureVlaSize sites that read arrayDeclSuffix's children are UNTOUCHED. It sits BEFORE arrayDeclSuffix in the SPECULATIVE suffix repeat (directDeclarator/abstractDirectDeclarator): `[*]` matches here; a real bound (`[N]`, or the deref-VLA `[*p]` whose `*p` is an expression) fails at the `]` / `*` position and the speculative loopEntry rolls back CLEANLY to arrayDeclSuffix (enabled by the schema-compiler honoring `speculative` on an inline repeat-alt). Semantics: an arrayStarSuffix suffix decays to a pointer EXACTLY like an absent-length `[]` for a parameter; a NON-parameter `[*]` is a constraint violation caught by the SAME paramDecay-gated S_ArrayParamQualifierNonParameter (0xE054).

typeSpecifierSeq.$comment: FC3 c1: C 6.7.2 type-specifier run — one-or-more specifier KEYWORDS in any order (`unsigned long long int` == `long long unsigned int` == `long unsigned long`). The inner alt is INLINE (no named wrapper rule) so the matched keyword TOKENS land as typeSpecifierSeq's DIRECT children — the semantic engine's multiset arm requires all-token children. Which multisets are VALID (and what core each resolves to, per data model) is the semantics.typeSpecifiers table; invalid combos (`unsigned float`, `short long`) parse fine here and reject SEMANTICALLY by absence from the table (S_InvalidTypeSpecifierCombination). The seq SUBSUMES the former single-token Void/Int/Long/Char alts (a one-keyword run). Keyword-led, so castExpr's commitRequiresTypeName triage rule 1 (not-a-lone-identifier) always commits a seq-led cast type. D-CSUBSET-BITINT: the `bitIntSpecifier` rule (`_BitInt(N)`) joins the element alt so `unsigned _BitInt(40)` parses as ONE run [unsigned-token, bitIntSpecifier-node] — keyword-led + FIRST-disjoint, so it only adds `_BitInt` to the element FIRST set (no speculation). A run CONTAINING a bitIntSpecifier is NOT all-tokens → the multiset arm skips it and the semantic engine's bitInt arm (semantics.bitInt) resolves it, scanning the sibling signedness tokens.

typeBase.$comment: The extern-declaration type-base (consumed ONLY by typeRef ← externDecl). D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-AGGREGATE-TYPE (TF arc C6, SQLite testfixture 2026-07-17): admits the struct/union/enum aggregate specifiers so a file-scope `extern struct Foo g;` / `extern union U g;` / `extern enum E g;` (and body/const/pointer forms) parses — the form sqliteInt.h uses at file scope (`extern SQLITE_WSD struct Sqlite3Config sqlite3Config;`, SQLITE_WSD→empty in the default no-WSD build), which 19 of the 44 sqlite `src/test*.c` TUs hit FIRST. Before this, `extern struct` P0009'd (`static struct`/plain/typedef already worked — the extern path was the sole gap). Mirrors `typeBaseAllowingStruct` (the struct-FIELD type-base) — the two stay separate named rules because their WRAPPERS differ: `typeRef` (extern) keeps a `{repeat StarOp}` pointer slot + a leading `{optional ConstKeyword}`, while `typeRefAllowingStruct` (field) drops the stars (they bind per-declarator) and uses a `{repeat headQualifier}` (const|volatile) prefix. SPECULATIVE + lookahead mirror the sibling + topLevelCompositeSpec: the composite FIRSTs (Struct/Union/EnumKeyword) are disjoint from the keyword/ident/typeof arms → unique-survivor direct descent in practice (the flag is defensive, moot). The type RESOLVER dispatches on the composite node's OWN rule (structSpec/unionSpec/enumSpec isTagReference / definition arms), NOT on externDecl, so an aggregate extern flows through the IDENTICAL resolution as `static struct Foo g;` — zero semantic/HIR change.

typeBaseAllowingStruct.$comment: FC7 D-FC7-NESTED-STRUCT-FIELD: the trailing `Identifier` alt admits a typedef-name (user struct/union type) as a struct/union FIELD type — symmetric with `typeBase` above, which already carries it. A struct/union body has no expression-statement context, so a bare Identifier in field-type position is UNAMBIGUOUSLY a type-name (no binder-sketch guard needed); a non-type name fails loud at semantic scope-lookup. Tried last, so keyword/tag/enum forms keep priority. D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION: also admits the struct/union/enum BODY forms (an inline nested definition `struct Inner { int x; } in;` as a field) — SPECULATIVE (mirror typedefHead), body-before-ref. This makes the body-in-head form RECURSIVE: a structSpecifierBody contains structFields, each of which may itself BE a structSpecifierBody (arbitrary nesting), the recursion falling out of the grammar — single-level speculation at each structField, never nested.

typeRefAllowingStruct.$comment: c23 (D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR): the struct/union member-head TYPE-SPECIFIER (base type + cv-qualifiers ONLY — NO pointer stars). The head qualifier slot is `{repeat headQualifier}` (= const|volatile) so a member head admits `volatile int isInterrupted;` (the SQLite case) and `const volatile`; mirrors the head-qualifier slots on topLevelHead/declHead/kwDeclHead. ★ The prior `{repeat StarOp}` slot was REMOVED: with the c23 member-declarator LIST, pointer stars MUST bind PER-DECLARATOR (inside each slot's `declarator` pointerLayer) — a head star applies to ALL declarators, so `int *a, b;` would wrongly type b as a pointer too (the silent layout miscompile c23 fixes). Each slot's `declarator` now owns its stars, exactly like every other declaration position (params/locals/globals). A head-position volatile FOLLOWED by a declarator `*` is a pointer-to-volatile-POINTEE — still rejected loud S_VolatilePointeeNotSupported by the per-declarator typing arm (head volatile + declarator star), so the reject stays complete. The trailing `{optional ConstKeyword}` is east const on the BASE (`int const`, west of any declarator star); east const on a pointer (`int * const`) rides the declarator's ptrQualifier.

varDecl.$comment: The COMMITTED local declaration statement — its FIRST set has NO Identifier (the bare-typedef-name form lives in declOrExprStmt). Branch 1: storage-class-specifier-led (`register T x;` — the specifier commits the decl, so the head admits the typedef-name arm); branch 2: qualifier-or-keyword-led (`const T x;` / `int x;`). Inline branches keep the children flat: [localDeclSpecifiers?, head, initDeclaratorList?, ;] — the row's specifierPrefix strip makes head=0/list=1 stable.
varDecl.$c28Comment: c28 D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-TYPE-DEFINITION: the `initDeclaratorList` is OPTIONAL — mirroring the top-level `topLevelDecl` (init-declarator-list?). A bare BLOCK-SCOPED struct/union/enum DEFINITION with no declarator (`struct Sublist { int nList; ht_slot *aList; };` as a STATEMENT inside a function — sqlite3.c:68508 walMergesort) routes through the kwDeclHead branch (Struct/Union/EnumKeyword-led → the unified c25 structSpec/unionSpec/enumSpec defines the type via `definesWhenChild`, minting the tag into the ENCLOSING BLOCK scope), takes NO declarator, and ends at EndStatement. A NON-defining no-declarator local (`int;`) parses + types but FAILS LOUD at HIR lowering (S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing) — the SAME tier + diagnostic as the top-level no-declarator path (CST→HIR's lowerVarLikeInto: no named declarator AND no composite specifier in the head ⇒ declares nothing). identVarDecl's list is deliberately NOT made optional (a bare typedef-name `T;` must stay an expression statement, NOT a no-declarator decl); only the keyword/specifier-led varDecl needs it (the struct/union/enum definition is keyword-led).

autoInferredVarDecl.$comment: FC17.5 (D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE, C23 6.7.9): the HEAD-LESS initializer-inferred declaration statement (`auto y = 42;`). NO declHead — the pre-existing varDecl path can never parse this form (declHead's bare-Identifier alt greedily reads `y` as a TYPE name → P0009). The specifier prefix is REQUIRED (localDeclSpecifiers is non-nullable) and semantically GATED: the declaration row's `requiredSpecifierToken: AutoKeyword` makes the Pass-1.5 inference arm fail LOUD when no `auto` is present, so C89 implicit-int shapes (`static x = 5;` / `register y = 2;` / `alignas(4) z = 9;` / `[[maybe_unused]] w = 3;`) that parse into this rule STAY errors — pinned. Probed FIRST in declOrAttrStmt (see its $fc175Comment for the C2 order rationale): it fast-fails (≤3 tokens) on every real-typed declaration (`static int x` — `int` is not a declarator), so ordinary decls pay one cheap probe only when specifier-led.

autoInferredForDecl.$comment: FC17.5 (D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE): the for-init mirror of autoInferredVarDecl — same shape MINUS the `;` (the for grammar owns it), exactly as forDecl mirrors varDecl. `for (auto i = 0; …)` is VALID C23 (6.8.5p3 admits auto in a for-init). Its declaration row copies forDecl's StaticKeyword gatedMarker so `for (static auto i = 0;;)` stays gated loud (6.8.5p3 violation).

identVarDecl.$comment: The bare-typedef-name declaration statement (`T x, *p;`). The PreferType commit guard (bare-string form — the FC2 triage): sketch-known Type commits, known Value rolls back (`a * b;` stays a multiplication), unknown runs the follower-operator test (`T x;` commits — `x` continues no value reading; `a * b;` rolls back — `*` does) and records the cross-file-oracle candidate on rollback.

declOrExprStmt.$comment: The C statement ambiguity site: FC5 prepends labelStmt (`Identifier Colon …` — a 2-token commit point disjoint from identVarDecl/exprStmt, neither of which has Colon in 2nd position), then identVarDecl BEFORE exprStmt (declared probe order is authoritative — the FC4 c0 fix). lookahead 256 (= 4096-token probe budget, vs the operand alt's 64): the identVarDecl probe must swallow an entire declaration INCLUDING initializer expressions and brace-init lists before its frame closes.

gotoStmt.$comment: D-CSUBSET-COMPUTED-GOTO: `goto label;` (C 6.8.6.1) OR the GNU extension `goto *expr;` (indirect branch to a computed code address). Two alternatives, mirroring sizeofExpr's 2-alt triage. The leading `*` is consumed as GOTO-STATEMENT syntax — so `gotoIndirect`'s operand is the pointer expression `expr` itself (NOT a deref), distinct from the prec-90 prefix `*`. `gotoLabel` keeps the FC5 plain form; the label-vs-indirect choice is unambiguous on the token AFTER `goto` (Identifier+EndStatement is a label; StarOp begins an indirect target). Both lower in cst_to_hir (gotoLabel→GotoStmt, gotoIndirect→IndirectGotoStmt).

labelStmt.$c31Comment: D-CSUBSET-LABEL-BUDGET-CLIFF (p19 Cluster G c31): the shape STAYS `Identifier Colon statement` so a label stays correct in every context, incl. a braceless control-flow body (`if(x) L: s;` — `s` IS the if's body). `commitAfterPrefix` is a PEG cut: after `Identifier Colon` no other declOrExprStmt alt (identVarDecl/exprStmt — neither has Colon in 2nd position) can match, so once the rule's fixed leading token-prefix is consumed in the probe (predictivePrefixLen = 3: `Identifier`, `Colon`, plus the first `statement` token — one harmless extra token, still far under budget) the parser COMMITS and parses the (arbitrarily large) `statement` child NON-speculatively, off the lookahead*16 probe budget. Without it a labeled statement >4096 tokens (sqlite3.c:213922 `json_parse_restart:` before a ~900-case switch) exhausts the budget → rollback → exprStmt → P0001 'got :'.

attributeDeclaration.$comment: FC17 (D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-STATEMENT, C23 6.8.1): the BARE attribute-declaration statement — `[[fallthrough]];` / `__attribute__((fallthrough));` — one-or-more attribute specifiers followed by `;`. REUSES `compositeAttrList` verbatim (audit-verified side-effect-free: `scanCompositePacked` runs ONLY from a structSpec/unionSpec node, never from a statement). SEMANTICS: the construct is a no-op at runtime — its hirLowering row maps to Skip (the emptyStmt precedent, zero C++); the semantic tier (pass2Post's bareStatementRule arm) runs the attribute scan over it for the UNKNOWN-standard-attribute warning only (facts discarded — no symbol to mark). DSS emits no fallthrough-placement warning to suppress, so `[[fallthrough]]` is semantics-neutral by construction (design note F7).

declOrAttrStmt.$comment: FC17 (D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-STATEMENT): the varDecl-vs-attributeDeclaration ambiguity site. A statement led by `[[` (stdAttr) or `__attribute__` (attrSpec) is EITHER an attributed declaration (`[[maybe_unused]] int x = 5;` — the attribute rides varDecl's localDeclSpecifiers prefix) OR a bare attribute-declaration statement (`[[fallthrough]];`) — genuinely colliding FIRSTs, so the alt is SPECULATIVE (declared order authoritative, the declOrExprStmt precedent); the bare form rolls back on the immediate `;`. lookahead 256 (= the 4096-token probe budget declOrExprStmt uses): a decl probe must swallow an entire declaration incl. initializers. HOT PATH (design-audit confirmed): every statement lead in EXACTLY ONE branch's FIRST (`int`/`const`/typedef-name/... — varDecl the sole survivor) takes the parser's unique-production DIRECT DESCENT (no probe, no checkpoint), so the wrapper adds zero speculation to ordinary statements. The wrapper MATERIALIZES a CST node (named alt rules do — the declOrExprStmt golden precedent): the statement>declOrAttrStmt>varDecl shape is pinned + the 2 consumers are transparent BY MECHANISM (HIR's unmapped-statement soleMeaningfulChild PassThrough peels it; semantic passes are rule-KEYED full-tree walks).
declOrAttrStmt.$fc175Comment: FC17.5 (D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE): `autoInferredVarDecl` joins the alt — ★ DECLARED FIRST (the design-audit's C2 MANDATORY order, NOT varDecl-first). Rationale: both decl rules' predictive prefix is 1 token, so a storage-class-led statement (`static …`) keeps 2 survivors and MUST probe; autoInferredVarDecl fast-fails within ~3 tokens on every real-typed declaration (after the specifier run, a type keyword is not a declarator), while a varDecl-FIRST order would probe varDecl under the 4096-token speculation budget and BUDGET-CLIFF existing valid `static const int big[] = {…>4096 tokens…}` declarations (sqlite3.c's largest block-scope static init is 3918 tokens = 96% of the budget — the sqlite gate would NOT catch the cliff). With autoInferred first, varDecl is the declared-LAST structural candidate for a specifier-led statement, so the parser's all-fail REPLAY (parser.cpp ~:1984) re-parses it NON-speculatively with NO budget — a genuine committed parse. Red-on-reorder pin: the >4096-token block-scope static-init test. The only dual-match shape `<specifiers> Ident ;` is invalid C either way — the diagnostic-shape pins (`auto T;` → initializer-required, `auto f(void);` → plain-identifier) hold the boundary. `auto int x;` (C89) still parses via varDecl on rollback — pinned.

localDeclSpecifier.$comment: Local storage-class specifiers + attributes. `static` (D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-STATIC) confers STATIC storage duration (C 6.2.4) — the varDecl row's linkageSpecifiers maps it to {binding:local, staticStorage:true} and CST→HIR routes the declaration to a hidden module-global; register/auto parse and are semantically inert (C: hints/default storage). `static` in a for-init is a C 6.8.5p3 violation, gated loud by the forDecl row. C11/C23 6.7.5 (D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS): `alignasSpec` joins the local-specifier alts too (a block-scope `alignas(16) int x;` is legal C) — FIRST {AlignasKeyword, AlignasLegacyKeyword} disjoint from the others, so the repeat stays predictively pruned; stripped from the child count (specifierPrefix: localDeclSpecifiers on varDecl) and ignored by the linkage scan. FC17 (D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR, C23 6.7.1): `constexpr` (ConstexprKeyword) joins the local alts too (a block-scope `constexpr int x = 5;` is legal C23, and forDecl reuses this prefix so `for (constexpr int i = 0; ...)` parses — VALID per C23 6.8.5p3, which admits auto/register/constexpr in a for-init) — FIRST {ConstexprKeyword} disjoint; a block-scope constexpr carries NO linkage (an automatic-storage constant), so the varDecl row IGNORES the keyword by kind (linkageSpecifierIgnoredKinds); the SEMANTIC constexpr validation reads it via semantics.constexpr.keywordToken.
localDeclSpecifier.$threadLocalComment: TLS C1 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL, C11 6.7.1p3): the `_Thread_local`/`thread_local` twin keywords join the LOCAL alts too — a block-scope `static thread_local int x;` is legal C (thread storage + static storage). FIRST disjoint (distinct exact-match keywords). The varDecl/autoInferredVarDecl rows map both spellings to {threadStorage:true} (Pass 1 marks isThreadLocal); a block-scope thread_local WITHOUT static/extern is the 6.7.1p3 constraint violation Pass 2 rejects (S_ThreadLocalRequiresStaticOrExtern). forDecl/autoInferredForDecl share this prefix, so `for (thread_local int i…)` PARSES — those rows gate the tokens loud (gatedMarkers → S_ThreadLocalRequiresStaticOrExtern; a for-init admits neither static nor extern, so the pairing can never be satisfied there).

param.$comment: FC4 c1: head + OPTIONAL declarator (named `int v`, pointer `const char *s`, abstract `int` / `int *` / `int (*)(int)`). The head admits a lone Identifier — C23 semantics: a lone identifier in param position is ALWAYS a type name (K&R identifier-lists were removed), so `int f(T)` is an unnamed param of type T (S_UnknownType when T isn't one).

statement.$c30Comment: c30 D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-TYPEDEF: `typedefDecl` is admitted as a block item (a BLOCK-SCOPED typedef — `typedef void(*LOGFUNC_t)(void*,int,const char*);` inside a function, sqlite3.c:187603). FIRST = TypedefKeyword, DISJOINT from every other statement alt (varDecl is qualifier/type-keyword/storage-class-led; declOrExprStmt is Identifier-led; the rest are their own keywords), so a unique-production DIRECT DESCENT — NO speculation. The whole typedef-name machinery is ALREADY scope-keyed: Pass-1 binds the alias into `current` (= the enclosing block scope) in the Ordinary namespace; the type resolver (`resolveTypeNodeImpl` SE5 arm) and the parse-time oracle (`BinderSketch`) both walk the scope chain, so a block-scoped typedef-name resolves IN the block, SHADOWS an outer same-name typedef, and does NOT leak out (red-on-disable pins enforce the non-leak). HIR→MIR's `lowerStmtNode` lowers a statement-position TypeDecl as a NO-OP (a typedef emits no code; mirrors the TOP-LEVEL TypeDecl). Mirrors c28 (block-scoped struct/union/enum definition).
statement.$fc17Comment: FC17 (D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-STATEMENT): `varDecl` is REPLACED by `declOrAttrStmt` (= alt{varDecl, attributeDeclaration}) — the ONLY varDecl-as-alt-branch site — so a `[[fallthrough]];` statement parses. A DIRECT statement-alt addition of attributeDeclaration was REFUTED: varDecl's FIRST already carries BracketOpen/AttributeKeyword (via localDeclSpecifiers' stdAttr/attrSpec), so the two branches collide → C_AmbiguousAlternatives at load. Every non-attribute-led statement still direct-descends (see declOrAttrStmt's hot-path note).

forStmt.$comment: FC4 c1: the init clause mirrors the statement-level decl split — committed keyword/qualifier/specifier-led forDecl vs the identifier-led forInitAmbig probe. Both are semicolon-LESS (the for grammar owns the `;`).
forStmt.$fc175Comment: FC17.5 (D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE): `autoInferredForDecl` joins the init alt — declared FIRST, the SAME C2 audit order as declOrAttrStmt (see its $fc175Comment; the >4096-token budget-cliff rationale applies identically to a for-init). The speculative/lookahead flags ride the OPTIONAL (the D-PARSE-SPECULATIVE-OPTIONAL shape — the enumTypeSpecifier precedent): the optional's own AltChoice is the DECISION POINT (its candidate enumeration flattens the inner alt's rules and EXCLUDES the skip branch), so flags on the inner alt object would never be consulted — empirically, `for (constexpr int i = 0;;)` first-match-committed the inference rule and P0001'd on `int` until the flags moved out here (the ConstexprInForInitAccepted pin caught it). No named wrapper, so the CST shape is UNCHANGED: forStmt's init child is still the forDecl/autoInferredForDecl/forInitAmbig node directly. Ordinary for-inits keep cheap decisions (`for (int i…` — `int` is in forDecl's FIRST only → its probe is the sole survivor; `for (i…` — Identifier → forInitAmbig), the multi-survivor probe fires only on a specifier-led init (`for (auto i…` / `for (static …` / `for (constexpr …`). An ABSENT init (`for (;;)`) rides the speculative optional's nullable-skip arm (parser.cpp ~:1941) exactly as before.

sehTryStmt.$comment: c115 (SEH arc 2/3): MSVC `__try compound-statement (__except '(' expr ')' compound-statement | __finally compound-statement)` — learn.microsoft.com/cpp/cpp/try-except-statement. FIRST = {TryKeyword}, disjoint from every other statement alt -> unique-production direct descent, NON-speculative; the except/finally fork is disambiguated one level down by sehHandler's own disjoint FIRSTs (ExceptKeyword vs FinallyKeyword). Both bodies REUSE the `block` rule — MSVC requires compound statements (braces), so the grammar enforces it by construction. The filter expression is an ordinary `expression`; its special context (where _exception_code()/_exception_info() are legal) is a SEMANTIC property checked at CST->HIR lowering + the HIR verifier, not a grammar one. __finally parses (so the diagnostic is a positioned lowering fail-loud citing D-CSUBSET-SEH-FINALLY, not a raw P0001 cascade) but has NO consumer (sqlite ground-truth: zero uses) — trigger-gated.

sehLeaveStmt.$comment: c115: `__leave ;` parses (FIRST = {LeaveKeyword}, disjoint) so the diagnostic is a positioned lowering fail-loud citing D-CSUBSET-SEH-LEAVE rather than a parse cascade. NO consumer (sqlite: zero uses) — trigger-gated; the lowering rejects it.

assignmentExpr.$comment: FC5: a C `assignment-expression` — every operator EXCEPT the comma (the two-level expression gate). minPrecedence 15 folds assignment (prec 15) and everything tighter but STOPS before the comma operator (prec 10, looser), so a `,` at a separator site (argList / initValue / enumerator) is left for the surrounding rule's separator, never swallowed as a comma-operator. The top-level `expression` rule (minPrec 0) keeps the comma operator for expr-statements / for-clauses / return / parens / index / conditions.

operand.$lookaheadComment: FC2 Part A: raised 8 -> 64. The speculation budget is lookahead*16 tokens per probe (parser SpeculationProbe); a castExpr probe must consume the ENTIRE cast operand expression before its frame closes, so the old 128-token budget was a correctness cliff for casts of long expressions (and for large compound-literal brace inits). 64 -> 1024-token probe budget.

compoundLiteralExpr.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c3 (D-CSUBSET-COMPOUND-LITERAL-TYPEDEF): the type position is castTypeRef (typedef-admitting via its bare-Identifier base alt) with the SAME commitRequiresTypeName binder triage castExpr uses. The `{` follower makes most forms unambiguous (triage case 4: BlockOpen is not a continuable value-reading operator -> COMMIT for unknown names); a KNOWN-value identifier `(a){...}` rolls back to the value reading, whose `(a)` parenExpr then hits the orphan `{` -> LOUD parse error (C says the form is invalid anyway).

sizeofExpr.$comment: FC6: `sizeof ( type-name )` | `sizeof unary-expression` (C 6.5.3.4). The type form reuses FC2's castTypeRef + commitRequiresTypeName binder triage — `sizeof(int)` / `sizeof(struct S)` / `sizeof(char*)` commit as types; `sizeof(x)` for a value `x` rolls back to the value form (a parenExpr operand); `sizeof x` (no parens) is the value form directly. The result type is size_t (U64); the operand is UNEVALUATED — only its type reaches the SizeOf node.

alignofType.$comment: C11/C23 6.5.3.4: `_Alignof ( type-name )` / `alignof ( type-name )`. TYPE-NAME FORM ONLY — unlike sizeof there is NO value form (`_Alignof(expr)` is a constraint violation, caught by the type-binder). Reuses FC2's shared castTypeRef + commitRequiresTypeName binder triage (so `_Alignof(int)` / `_Alignof(struct S)` commit the parens as a type; a non-type name fails loud at type-resolve, never silently treated as a value). Single form → NO speculative/lookahead. Result type is size_t (U64); folds to the type's alignment via the type_layout engine, exactly like sizeof reads size.

typeofSpecifier.$comment: C23 6.7.2.5 (D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF): `typeof`/`typeof_unqual` — a TYPE-SPECIFIER whose meaning is the operand's type (typeof preserves top-level qualifiers, typeof_unqual strips them). Two operand forms, disambiguated SPECULATIVELY (parallel to sizeofExpr's type-vs-value alt): the TYPE-NAME form (`typeof(int)`/`typeof(volatile char)`/`typeof(int*)`) probes FIRST, and a VALUE operand (`typeof(x)`/`typeof(a.b)`) rolls back to the value form. FIRST-set-safe: the two brand-new disjoint keyword tokens make a `typeof` lead select this specifier unambiguously in every alt-list it is appended to — the inner alt is where the type-vs-value ambiguity lives, so the speculation budget applies only once a `typeof(` is already committed. The wrapper materializes a `typeofSpecifier` node containing the chosen `typeofType`/`typeofValue` (like sizeofExpr); the semantic tier resolves the inner form's operand and the HIR lowering recovers the stamped result via resolveStampedTypeBelow.

typeofType.$comment: C23 6.7.2.5 (D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF): the TYPE-NAME operand form — `typeof ( type-name )` / `typeof_unqual ( type-name )`. Reuses FC2's shared `castTypeRef` (so `typeof(int*)` / `typeof(volatile char)` / `typeof(MyTypedef)` all resolve) + the `commitRequiresTypeName` binder triage. POLARITY = `requireKnownType` (NOT sizeofType's PreferType default), mirroring `alignasTypeName`: a bare identifier commits the TYPE reading ONLY when it is a KNOWN typedef-name; an ENUM CONSTANT or other non-type identifier (binder-sketch Unknown) rolls back to `typeofValue` and is typed as an expression — the C 6.7.6.3p11-style disambiguation (under PreferType a bare `typeof(ENUM_CONST)` would wrongly commit as a type → a spurious S_UnknownType). A keyword-led type (`typeof(int)`) still commits (triage rule 1). The inline 2-keyword alt at child 0 mirrors alignofType (no wrapper) — visible-child 2 is the castTypeRef (same layout as sizeofType/alignofType).

typeofValue.$comment: C23 6.7.2.5 (D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF): the EXPRESSION operand form — `typeof ( expression )` / `typeof_unqual ( expression )` (reached when the type-name reading rolls back). The operand is the full comma-inclusive `expression` (C23-correct — `typeof(a, b)` takes the comma expression's type). UNEVALUATED (6.7.2.5p2): only the operand's TYPE reaches the semantic tier (via subtreeType), which strips top-level volatile for typeof_unqual; a bit-field operand is a constraint violation (S_TypeofBitfieldOperand). visible-child 2 is the expression (same keyword+paren layout as typeofType).

bitIntSpecifier.$comment: C23 6.2.5/6.7.2 (D-CSUBSET-BITINT): the `_BitInt ( constant-expression )` bit-precise integer TYPE-SPECIFIER — structurally the typeofType twin (keyword + '(' + operand + ')'), a PLAIN sequence (NO speculative parsing: the distinct `_BitInt` keyword + the unambiguous `(` make it FIRST-set-disjoint from every other specifier). It is an ELEMENT of `typeSpecifierSeq` (NOT a standalone type-base alt), so `unsigned`/`signed` compose with it through the SAME C 6.7.2 specifier run, order-independently (`unsigned _BitInt(40)` == `_BitInt(40) unsigned`): the run lands the bitIntSpecifier NODE plus any signedness TOKENS as sibling children, and the semantic tier (semantics.bitInt) reads the width from `assignmentExpr` (child 2 — the comma-free const-expr rule bit-field widths / array dims / alignas / static_assert also use), scans the run for a signedness token (DEFAULT signed), const-folds + validates N (0xE04A-0xE04D + the C1 N>64 gate), and interns `bitInt(N, signed)`. A run mixing `_BitInt` with a NON-signedness specifier (`int _BitInt(4)`) rejects as S_InvalidTypeSpecifierCombination.

alignasSpec.$comment: C11/C23 6.7.5 (D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS): the `_Alignas ( … )` / `alignas ( … )` alignment SPECIFIER — a DECLARATION-SPECIFIER (NOT an expression operand), so it is a SEQUENCE (its tree node is `alignasSpec`, which the specifier-prefix mechanism matches in singleDeclSpecifier/localDeclSpecifier/structMemberDeclSpecifiers). Two spellings: the reserved `_Alignas` (AlignasKeyword) and the C23 `alignas` (AlignasLegacyKeyword). The parenthesized operand is `alignasArg` — EITHER a type-name (`alignas(int)` contributes _Alignof(int)) OR a constant-expression (`alignas(16)`). SEMANTICS live in the semantic tier (semantics.alignas): the value/type is computed + validated (power-of-two, ≤256, ≥ the declared type's natural alignment) + STORED on the SymbolRecord (variables) / fed into the struct's fieldAligns (members). The specifier is STRIPPED before positional-child counting (decl_prefix_strip.hpp) so name/type indices stay stable, and IGNORED by the linkage scan (linkageSpecifierIgnoredRules) — exactly like `__attribute__`.

alignasArg.$comment: C11/C23 6.7.5 (D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS): the operand of an `alignas` specifier — the TYPE form (`alignasTypeName`, e.g. `alignas(int)` / `alignas(struct S)`) OR the VALUE form (a constant-expression, `assignmentExpr` — the SAME comma-free const-expr rule staticAssertDecl's condition + a bit-field width + an array dimension use, so `alignas(16)` / `alignas(2*8)` / `alignas(ENUM_VAL)` all fold). SPECULATIVE, with the type branch carrying the `commitRequiresTypeName` binder triage: the operand position is genuinely type-vs-value AMBIGUOUS (`alignas(int)` type vs `alignas(ENUM_VAL)` value). The guard lives on the `alignasTypeName` BRANCH (not on this alt) — the parser's triage is keyed off the PROBED branch rule, exactly like `operand`'s `castExpr` branch. The semantic tier discriminates the committed reading by the child rule (alignasTypeName ⇒ type form → _Alignof(T); else value form → const-eval).

alignasTypeName.$comment: C11/C23 6.7.5 (D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS): the TYPE-form branch of an `alignas` operand — a lone `castTypeRef` wrapped in a SEQUENCE so it can carry `commitRequiresTypeName` (the guard must sit on the speculative BRANCH the parser probes; a bare `castTypeRef` alt-branch would be probed as `castTypeRef` and `typeNameCommitRule(castTypeRef)` is not set → the guard would never fire). POLARITY = `requireKnownType` (NOT the PreferType default): a bare identifier commits the TYPE reading ONLY when it is a KNOWN typedef-name (sketch/oracle Type); an ENUM CONSTANT or any non-type identifier (sketch Unknown) rolls back to the VALUE reading and const-folds — the C 6.7.6.3p11-style disambiguation. Under PreferType a bare enum-constant `alignas(AL)` would wrongly commit as a type (no follower operator) → a spurious S_AlignasNonConstant. A keyword-led type (`alignas(int)`) still commits (triage rule 1).

labelAddressExpr.$comment: D-CSUBSET-COMPUTED-GOTO: the GNU `&&label` label-address expression. A DEDICATED operand rule (parallel to sizeofExpr) — NOT the prec-90 prefix `&` doubled — because its operand is a LABEL name in the label namespace, NOT a value: routing it through the ordinary identifier-operand path would resolve `label` as an (undefined) variable and fail to parse. The `Identifier` here is a RAW token (like labelStmt's), recovered by the CST→HIR lowering which resolves it to the label's per-function ordinal and stamps the result `void*`. The leading `&&` is the single AndAndOp token (no lexer change).

vaStartExpr.$comment: FC12a-core (D-FC12A-VARIADIC-CALLEE): `va_start(ap, last)`. Takes the `va_list` lvalue (assignmentExpr — the comma-free, non-comma-operator form) and an OPTIONAL second `last` arg that is parsed-and-ignored (the SysV gp/fp offsets derive from the FnSig, not from `last`). A leaf form in the operand alternative list, parallel to sizeofExpr.

vaArgExpr.$comment: FC12a-core (D-FC12A-VARIADIC-CALLEE): `va_arg(ap, T)`. The SECOND argument is a TYPE — it reuses FC2's shared `castTypeRef` rule + `commitRequiresTypeName` binder triage so `va_arg(ap, int)` / `va_arg(ap, MyTypedef)` commit the type position as a type (and `va_arg(ap, x)` for a VALUE x fails loud at the type-resolve, never silently treating a value as a type). The dedicated HIR lowering recovers T from the semantic stamp, exactly like sizeof.

vaEndExpr.$comment: FC12a-core (D-FC12A-VARIADIC-CALLEE): `va_end(ap)`. Takes the `va_list` lvalue; a no-op teardown in the SysV model.

genericExpr.$comment: FC16 (D-CSUBSET-GENERIC-SELECTION): C11/C23 6.5.1.1 `_Generic ( controlling-expression , generic-assoc-list )` — a PRIMARY EXPRESSION whose value+type IS the selected association's expression. A leaf in the operand alternative list (parallel to vaArgExpr). The controlling expression is `assignmentExpr` (comma-free — a `,` here separates associations, so the comma-operator form must not swallow it, exactly like vaArgExpr's ap arg). The assoc-list is a NON-EMPTY comma-separated list of `type-name : assignment-expression` (genericAssoc) or `default : assignment-expression` (genericDefaultAssoc); the list shape (first + `{repeat {, item}}`) mirrors initDeclaratorList. Each genericAssoc's type position is `castTypeRef` + the SAME `commitRequiresTypeName` binder triage va_arg/casts use, so `int:`/`MyTypedef:` commit as a TYPE and a VALUE in type position fails loud at the type-resolve (S_UnknownType). SELECTION is compile-time in the SEMANTIC tier (semantics.generic): the controlling expr's type (after lvalue conversion — top-level volatile stripped) is matched against each association's resolved type for COMPATIBILITY (interned TypeId equality); EXACTLY ONE must match, else the `default` — a no-match-no-default is S_GenericSelectionNoMatch and >1 match is S_GenericSelectionAmbiguous (both unsuppressable constraint violations). Pass 2 stamps the genericExpr node with the WINNER's result type + records the winning association's expression node; the dedicated HIR lowering (hirLowering.genericRule → lowerGeneric) lowers ONLY that sub-expression (its type + value), discarding the rest (the non-selected expressions are UNEVALUATED per 6.5.1.1p3, imposing no constraints beyond being syntactically valid).

genericAssoc.$comment: FC16 (D-CSUBSET-GENERIC-SELECTION): ONE `type-name : assignment-expression` OR `default : assignment-expression` association of a `_Generic`. The `default` form is a distinct alt (genericDefaultAssoc) so the DefaultKeyword lead is unambiguous; otherwise the type position is `castTypeRef` (typedef-admitting, with the shared `commitRequiresTypeName` triage — a value in type position fails loud). The two alts share the `: assignmentExpr` tail shape; the semantic tier discriminates default-vs-typed by the DefaultKeyword child.

staticAssertDecl.$comment: C11/C23 6.7.10 `_Static_assert(constant-expression, string-literal);` and the C23 1-arg `_Static_assert(constant-expression);` — a static-assertion DECLARATION valid at BOTH file scope (topLevel alt) AND block scope (statement alt). Two spellings: the reserved `_Static_assert` and the C23 `static_assert` (both keywords). The condition is `assignmentExpr` (comma-free — a `,` here separates the optional message, so the comma-operator form must not swallow it, exactly like vaStartExpr's ap arg). The message is an OPTIONAL adjacent-string-concat literal (the vaStartExpr optional-second-arg pattern; ABSENT = the C23 1-arg form).
