SEMANTICS live in the semantic tier (semantics.staticAssertRule): the condition is const-evaluated by the SAME evaluator that folds `sizeof(T)`/enum/arithmetic in an array dimension — ZERO ⇒ S_StaticAssertFailed (message = the string literal), NONZERO ⇒ nothing. The `hirKind: Skip` row lowers a PASSED assertion to nothing in BOTH positions (the emptyStmt/includeDirective Skip precedent), and — because it is a MAPPED rule — makes peelToCore STOP here (never descending into the sole condition child of the 1-arg form).

asmStmt.$comment: FC17.9(i) D-CSUBSET-INLINE-ASM: the GNU inline-asm STATEMENT, cycle-1 = the empty-template optimizer barrier. `__asm__ [volatile] ( <string-literal-template> ) ;` — a structural CLONE of staticAssertDecl (keyword-led, consumes `(` a payload `)` `;`). FIRST = AsmKeyword, DISJOINT from every other statement alt → unique-production DIRECT DESCENT, NON-speculative (the sehTryStmt/TryKeyword precedent). The template is captured as stringLiteralExpr (adjacent-concat + prefixes); the semantic tier (semantics.inlineAsmRule) decodes it and REQUIRES a strictly-empty decoded string, else S_InlineAsmNonEmptyTemplate (D-CSUBSET-INLINE-ASM-TEXT) — a non-empty template carries real per-target instructions we cannot yet emit, so silently lowering it to a no-op barrier would be a miscompile. The optional `volatile` (already VolatileKeyword) is accepted but SEMANTICALLY INERT in cycle-1 (a no-output asm is implicitly volatile — GCC 6.47.2.1 — so the empty form is always a barrier). Operand/clobber lists (`: out : in : clobbers`) are NOT admitted here (the rule ends at `)`) → they fail loud at parse (P_UnexpectedToken on the `:`), the named deferral D-CSUBSET-INLINE-ASM-OPERANDS; `asm goto` (D-CSUBSET-INLINE-ASM-GOTO) and the bare `asm`/`__volatile__` spellings (D-CSUBSET-INLINE-ASM-SPELLING) are likewise deferred (the grammar admits only `__asm__`). The hirLowering row (asmStmt → InlineAsm) lowers it to MirOpcode::CompilerBarrier (the _ReadWriteBarrier op — zero target instructions, full-memory fence).

castTypeRef.$comment: c27 D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-POINTEE: the two qualifier slots are `{repeat headQualifier}` (const|volatile, either order) — admitting a volatile-qualified cast type-name `(volatile u32 **)p` (sqlite 67392). The resolver wraps the base in VolatileQual when a volatile precedes the stars, so the cast builds Ptr<Ptr<VolatileQual(u32)>> and a deref of it is volatile; const stays ignored. (Was two `{optional ConstKeyword}` slots pre-c27.) c11 D-CSUBSET-EAST-CONST-CAST: a cast/sizeof type-name admits const PRE-base (`const char *` — west) AND POST-base before the stars (`char const *` — EAST, the SQLite-pervasive form). In a DECLARATION the `*` lives in the declarator so the head `char const` already parsed; a cast bundles base+stars here, so the post-base const slot is what admits east-const. The const resolver IGNORES const for type identity (a cast yields an rvalue), so all forms intern the same pointer type. The two const slots straddle the REQUIRED base token (no ambiguity); a POST-stars qualifier (`char * const` / `u32 * volatile`) RIDES INSIDE the pointer layer (c29 D-CSUBSET-POST-STAR-CAST-QUALIFIER): the leading stars are `{repeat pointerLayer}` (= `StarOp {repeat ptrQualifier}`, c-subset ~500) NOT bare `{repeat StarOp}`, so the post-star const/volatile is present ONLY with a star — NO zero-star adjacency (the c11 ambiguity a separate post-stars optional would have caused). The resolver counts each pointerLayer child as ONE star for ptrDepth and STRIPS its ptrQualifiers: a cast yields an RVALUE with NO top-level cv (C 6.5.4) — `(int * const)p` and `(int *)p` intern the SAME `Ptr<int>`, and a post-star VOLATILE is dropped (no VolatileQual on the cast POINTER) — the c27 PRE-stars-volatile pointee path (`volatile u32 *`→`Ptr<VolatileQual(u32)>`) is SEPARATE and unbroken. c26 D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-DECLARATOR-TYPE-NAME: an ABSTRACT direct-declarator (`(*)(void)`/`(*)(int)`/`[N]`/`(*)[N]`) may follow the base+stars — the fn-pointer/array type-name forms (`(int(*)(void))p`, the sqlite osGeteuid cast). It reuses `abstractDirectDeclarator` (the abstract twin of `directDeclarator`, sharing parenDeclarator+fnSuffix+arrayDeclSuffix), so the semantic resolver folds the type via the SHARED `directDeclaredType` (Ptr<Fn(void)->int>) — the SAME machinery a declaration's declarator takes. It is the ABSTRACT twin (Identifier base EXCLUDED) NOT `directDeclarator` because a bare-identifier base would make `(c * c)` (a multiplication) speculatively commit as a bogus cast; and it is NOT the full `declarator` because the leading POINTER STARS are consumed by `{repeat pointerLayer}` above (a star-led declarator's FIRST `pointerLayer` would collide with the repeat — C_AmbiguousAlternatives). OPTIONAL: a star-only cast (`int*`) leaves it empty — none of {fnSuffix,parenDeclarator,arrayDeclSuffix} matches the closing `)`, so `(int*)` stays `castTypeBase`+pointerLayer(StarOp). A NAMED declarator with the name NESTED in parens (`(int (x))expr` / `(int(*p)(void))expr`) parses (via parenDeclarator) but is rejected LOUD at the type-resolve via `S_TypeNameDeclaratorNotAbstract` — never silently parsed as the bare base.

charLiteralExpr.$comment: C11/C23 6.4.4.4: a character constant is opener + coalesced body. The opener is an INLINE token alt (`'`|`L'`|`u'`|`U'`|`u8'`) — token-leaf alt branches consume EXACTLY one token and push it DIRECTLY (no wrapper node; the stringLiteralExpr precedent), so the opener token stays a DIRECT child of this node and charLiteralNodeOf / the semantic char-typing override find it AND recover its SPECIFIC kind (the C23 element-type discriminator). Unprefixed `'x'` is byte-identical to the pre-CycleB `[CharStart, CharLiteral]` rule.

stringLiteralExpr.$comment: C 5.1.1.2 phase 6 (D-CSUBSET-ADJACENT-STRING-CONCAT): adjacent string literals concatenate — `"a" "b"` is `"ab"`. The first opener+StringLiteral pair is required; the zero-or-more repeat captures each further pair as FLAT children (sequences inline, no wrapper node), so a lone string yields exactly 2 children, byte-identical to the pre-c20 single-pair rule. The opener is an INLINE token alt (C11/C23 6.4.5: `"`|`L"`|`u"`|`U"`|`u8"`) — token-leaf alt branches consume EXACTLY one token and push it DIRECTLY (no wrapper node; the typeSpecifierSeq precedent), so the opener token stays a DIRECT child of this node and stringLiteralNodeOf / decodeAdjacentStringBodies find it AND recover its SPECIFIC kind (the element-core discriminator). Concatenation is at the DECODED code-unit level (each body decoded FIRST, then joined) via the single chokepoint every body consumer routes through. Safe in every use site: primaryExpr, attr args (terminate at `)`), externFuncTail (terminates at `;`) — none has a string opener in its follow set. Prefix-mixing across adjacent pieces (`u"a" L"b"`) is a LATER cycle — the element core is taken from the FIRST opener today.

argList.$comment: FC5: assignmentExpr (NOT expression) — the comma here is an arg SEPARATOR, never a comma-operator, so `f(a, b)` is 2 args. A comma-operator arg is written parenthesized: `f((a, b))`.

semantics.$volatileMarkerComment: c21 (D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-QUALIFIER): the language's volatile qualifier token. Read by the type-position resolver's CO-LOCATED pointer arm (castTypeRef cast/sizeof type-names) to reject a pointer-to-volatile-POINTEE (`volatile int *`) position-aware (volatile-before-star = pointee = REJECT; after-the-star = pointer-object = ACCEPT). DECLARATION forms — objects, params, AND struct/union members (c23 removed the head star from typeRefAllowingStruct, so a member's `*` now binds per-declarator like every other declaration position) — reject via the per-declaration-rule volatileMarker (head-volatile + a declarator star); the two arms together make the fail-loud complete.
semantics.$atomicMarkerComment: FC17.9(d) cycle 1b (D-CSUBSET-ATOMIC): the language's `_Atomic`-class qualifier token — the LIVE SemanticConfig-level driver of the type-qualifier wrap, EXACTLY parallel to `volatileMarker` above. The type-position resolver (resolveTypeNodeImpl's CO-LOCATED base arm + declaratorDeclaredType's east/pointer-layer arm) reads THIS token: an `atomicMarker` BEFORE the first `pointerToken` wraps the base pointee (`_Atomic int *` => Ptr<atomicQualified(int)>); an `atomicMarker` INSIDE a pointer layer (east) wraps that pointer object (`int * _Atomic` => atomicQualified(Ptr<int>)). It COMPOSES with `volatileMarker` in the ONE shared {volatile,atomic} bitset skin (cycle 1a `qualified` merges bits, order-independent). NOTE: unlike volatile, `_Atomic` is a NEW qualifier with NO legacy per-DeclarationRule marker — object-atomicity is derived UNIFORMLY from the resolved TYPE (isAtomicQualified) at access lowering, exactly as c27 made volatile do (the per-DeclarationRule `volatileMarker` scan was RETIRED at c27; a parallel per-declaration atomicMarker would be write-only dead config, so it is deliberately NOT added). Source-agnostic: the engine reads THIS token, never a hardcoded keyword.
semantics.$externLibraryByFormatComment: FF6 Slice 2 + audit fold (2026-06-02): per-format runtime library identity for source-declared externs. compileSingleUnit step 2.5 populates HirAttribute<FfiMetadata> per ExternFunction/ExternGlobal by calling synthesizeFfiFromSourceDecls() with the entry whose key matches objectFormatKindName(format.kind()). Per-LANGUAGE field (not per-declaration-rule) — the post-fold #1 placement on DeclarationRule was fragile; the pipeline first-match-loop would silently pick the wrong rule on grammars with multiple extern-shaped decl rules. Library identities: PE → msvcrt.dll (Win32 C runtime for x86_64 hello-world); ELF → libc.so.6 (glibc on Linux); Mach-O → /usr/lib/libSystem.B.dylib (libSystem dispatcher publishes the libc surface). Per-symbol overrides (`extern "otherlib.dll" int foo();`) anchored D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-LIBRARY-SYNTAX.
semantics.$shippedLibDirsComment: FF11 (2026-06-05) + Model 3 (2026-06-09): the SYSTEM include search path (C's /usr/include analogue) for the angle form `#include <h>`. A directory under src/dss-config/; the header stem maps to a language-NEUTRAL JSON descriptor (`<stem>.json`, e.g. stdio.json) read by src/ffi/shipped_lib_descriptor.cpp — its symbols (name + hir-text signature) are injected into semantic scope before Pass 2, then flow through FF5 synthesizeFfiFromSourceDecls like a program's own extern. Model 3 made each descriptor PLATFORM-NEUTRAL: ONE descriptor per header, carrying a per-object-format `library` map ({"pe":...,"elf":...,"macho":...}); the active compilation target's format selects its runtime image at compile_pipeline resolution (keyed via objectFormatKindName), so a SINGLE neutral directory serves every target. This DISSOLVED the former per-platform-directory selection + D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-PLATFORM-SELECT. The full surface (stdio/stdlib/string/ctype/math) + provenance/variadic-exclusion notes live in src/dss-config/shippedLibs/README.md.
semantics.$pointerConversionsComment: D-LANG-POINTER-VOID-CONVERT (step 13.2, 2026-06-02): C-standard §6.3.2.3 — `void*` converts implicitly to/from any object-pointer type without a cast. The two directions are configured INDEPENDENTLY (matching C++'s split, where only the safe direction is implicit). c-subset declares BOTH true (C-family semantics). A future C++ frontend would declare {implicitToVoidPtr: true, implicitFromVoidPtr: false} matching ISO C++ §7.11. Rust/Swift would declare both false. DEFAULT is false (struct-init); strict typing is the safety-respecting default, opposite of the Array→Ptr decay opt-out direction (which is pervasive C-family idiom; void-ptr conversion is a safety relaxation). Pointer arithmetic on void* and void*↔fn-pointer conversions are separate concerns anchored at D-LANG-VOIDPTR-ARITH-REJECT and D-LANG-VOIDPTR-FN-CONVERT respectively.
semantics.$pointerAliasingComment: Two orthogonal C99-derived alias-analysis opt-ins. `strictAliasingOnDistinctTypes` (C99 §6.5): accessing an object through a pointer of an incompatible type is undefined behavior — lets the MIR optimizer's CSE/LICM Load admission prove Ptr<I32> and Ptr<I64> don't alias (Rule 6 in mirMayAlias). `charTypesAliasAll` (C99 §6.5 ¶7): character-type pointers may alias an object of any type — the canonical strict-aliasing carve-out for serializers/hash-visitors/memcpy. C-family languages declare both true. A Rust frontend or strict-typed DSL would declare `charTypesAliasAll: false` (the type-checker enforces aliasing at a higher tier). See D-OPT-LOAD-ALIAS-ANALYSIS for the substrate's design.
semantics.$declaratorsComment: FC4 c1 stage 2a: the C 6.7.6 declarator role vocabulary. The shared declarator walk (name extraction — binder sketch + analyzer) and the Pass 1.5 inversion engine consume ONLY these resolved roles. The pointer/fn/array/group structure lives in the DECLARATOR; heads carry the base type alone.

declarators.$arraySuffixModifierTokensComment: VLA C4c (D-CSUBSET-VLA, C99 §6.7.6.2/6.7.6.3): the token kinds that decorate an array-PARAMETER suffix — a `static`, the three cv-qualifiers, and the unspecified-size `*` — none of which is part of the length BOUND. The shared arraySuffixBoundNode / arraySuffixHasModifier helpers skip these (DIRECT children only) so a bound behind a decoration (`int a[static n]`) is still located, and a bare decoration (`int a[*]`, `int a[restrict]`) reads as an ABSENT bound (decays like `[]`) rather than a runtime VLA. ENGINE-AGNOSTIC: a language without array-parameter decorations simply omits this list (empty ⇒ the plain first-non-bracket-child view, unchanged).

semantics.$parametersComment: C 6.7.6.3p10: a parameter list of exactly `(void)` declares ZERO parameters. The engine applies this at the ONE param-harvest chokepoint when the sole param resolves to lattice Void AND is unnamed; a NAMED void param or void-mixed-with-others is S_InvalidVoidParam. Languages without the flag keep raw param lists.

declarations.$prototypeSynthesizesExternComment: c86 (D-CSUBSET-BARE-PROTO-EXTERN-SYNTHESIS): a BLOCK-scope bare prototype (`int f(int);` inside a body — re-homed onto the file scope per D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-PROTOTYPE, C 6.2.2p4) that survives with no in-TU definition synthesizes the same no-library extern as a file-scope proto (routed to the module decls, mirroring the static-local accumulator). Same three-way resolution as topLevelDecl's flag.
declarations.$staticStorageComment: D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-STATIC: a block-scope `static` confers static storage duration (C 6.2.4). CST→HIR's linkageFrom folds {binding:local, staticStorage:true} from the SAME prefix scan and routes the declaration to a hidden module-global (internal linkage). register/auto/attribute specifiers carry no linkage and are ignored (ignoredKinds/ignoredRules) so they never trip the unknown-specifier diagnostic.
declarations.$volatileComment: c27 (D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-POINTEE): `volatile` is a TYPE qualifier (TypeKind::VolatileQual). The resolver wraps the base in VolatileQual when a volatileMarker precedes the stars (`volatile int *` => Ptr<VolatileQual(int)>) and wraps the pointer for an east ptrQualifier (`int * volatile` => VolatileQual(Ptr<int>)); object-volatility is read off the symbol's top-level VolatileQual type at access lowering, pointee-volatility rides the accessed type at the deref/index/member (MirInstFlags::Volatile). ALL pointee/member/param/typedef forms now compile — the prior S_VolatilePointeeNotSupported reject is retired. const stays ignored (no wrapper).
declarations.$threadLocalLinkageComment: TLS C1 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL, C11 6.7.1p3): both thread-storage spellings map to {threadStorage:true} so the LEGAL block-scope form `static thread_local int x;` resolves cleanly (without these entries the tokens would fail loud H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier on legal C). threadStorage does NOT itself confer static routing — a bare block-scope `thread_local int x;` is rejected by Pass 2 (S_ThreadLocalRequiresStaticOrExtern) before lowering.
declarations.$noreturnIgnoreComment: FC16 (D-CSUBSET-NORETURN): the bare `_Noreturn` keyword carries NO linkage — ignored by kind (like RegisterKeyword/AutoKeyword). The attribute form `__attribute__((noreturn))` on a LOCAL is already covered by attrSpec ∈ linkageSpecifierIgnoredRules (wholesale). The SEMANTIC noreturn scan reads the keyword/attribute; without this ignore the `_Noreturn` token would fail loud H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier.
declarations.$constexprIgnoreComment: FC17 (D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR, C23 6.7.1): a BLOCK-scope `constexpr` carries NO linkage (it declares an automatic-storage constant object; only the FILE-scope form has C23 6.2.2p3 internal linkage — see topLevelDecl's linkageSpecifiers) — ignored by kind like RegisterKeyword/AutoKeyword. The SEMANTIC constexpr validation (semantics.constexpr.keywordToken) reads it; without this ignore the `constexpr` token would fail loud H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier at block scope.
declarations.$alignasIgnoreComment: C11/C23 6.7.5 (D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS): an `alignasSpec` in the local specifier prefix (`alignas(16) int x;`) carries NO linkage — its subtree (`_Alignas ( … )`) is skipped WHOLESALE by the linkage scan (exactly like the C23 `stdAttr` `[[…]]`). The alignment is read by the SEMANTIC alignas scan, not here. Without this the `_Alignas` keyword token would fail loud H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier.

declarations.$forStaticComment: D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-STATIC (MF-2): `static` parses in the shared localDeclSpecifiers prefix, but a static (or any non-auto/register) storage class in a for-statement init-declaration is a C 6.8.5p3 constraint violation — gate it LOUD (never silently lower as an automatic loop variable). NO linkageSpecifiers here: a static for-init must error, not route to a hidden global. The StaticKeyword gatedMarker stays; only the S_VolatileNotSupported gate is removed (volatile is implemented — c21). TLS C1 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): both thread-storage spellings parse in the shared prefix too — gated loud with S_ThreadLocalRequiresStaticOrExtern (C11 6.7.1p3: thread_local needs static/extern, and a for-init admits neither, so the requirement is unsatisfiable there; WITHOUT the gate `for (thread_local int i…)` would silently lower as a plain automatic — the row has no linkageSpecifiers, so the Pass-1 threadStorage scan finds no entry).

declarations.$comment: FC17.5 (D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE, C23 6.7.9): the HEAD-LESS initializer-inferred declaration. NO `head` — `inferTypeFromInitializer` waives the loader's declarator-mode head requirement and routes typing through the Pass-1.5 inference arm (single plain named declarator → initializer required → braced-single unwrap → infer → array/function DECAY → loud rejects for void/nullptr_t/unresolvable → stripVolatile → write). `declaratorList: 0` — post-strip children are [initDeclaratorList, EndStatement] (the specifierPrefix strip removes the REQUIRED localDeclSpecifiers prefix before positional counting; varDecl's is 1 because a declHead precedes its list). `requiredSpecifierToken: AutoKeyword` is the ★C1 presence gate — checked FIRST in the arm; without it `static x = 5;` (which PARSES into this rule) would silently become C89 implicit-int. ★M1: NO constMarker/volatileMarker — with no head, the const/volatile fallback scans span the WHOLE declaration INCLUDING THE INITIALIZER (the c58 initializer-const-leak: `auto p = (const char*)s;` would false-mark p const); C23 6.7.9 constrains auto to unqualified plain declarators anyway (`const auto x` is a loud parse error — D-CSUBSET-AUTO-QUALIFIED). NO prototypeSynthesizesExtern (a headless decl can never be a prototype; the plain-identifier gate rejects `auto f(void);` loud). linkage/ignored entries mirror varDecl so `static auto s = 1;` routes to a hidden module-global and `constexpr auto k = 5;` validates through the constexpr hook against the INFERRED type (P1 prefix scan → P1.5 infer → P2 validator ordering, audit-verified).
declarations.$threadLocalLinkageComment: TLS C1 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): mirror of varDecl's thread-storage entries — C23 6.7.1 admits `auto` beside thread_local, so `static thread_local auto s = 1;` is legal and must resolve; a bare `thread_local auto x = 5;` (no static) is Pass 2's S_ThreadLocalRequiresStaticOrExtern.

declarations.$comment: FC17.5 (D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE): the for-init mirror — same inference row as autoInferredVarDecl, but like forDecl it carries NO linkageSpecifiers (a static for-init must ERROR, not route to a hidden global) and COPIES forDecl's StaticKeyword gatedMarker so `for (static auto i = 0;;)` fails loud S_StaticStorageInForInit (C 6.8.5p3). TLS C1 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): copies forDecl's thread-storage gatedMarkers too — `for (thread_local auto i = 0;;)` fails loud S_ThreadLocalRequiresStaticOrExtern (6.7.1p3 is unsatisfiable in a for-init).

declarations.$arrayToPointerComment: c82 (D-CSUBSET-PARAM-ARRAY-ADJUSTMENT, C 6.7.6.3p7): a parameter declared 'array of T' — sized (`int a[64]`), unsized (`int a[]`), or multi-dim (`int a[][5]` -> ptr to int[5]) — ADJUSTS to 'pointer to T'. The flag (a) permits the absent-length `[]` on the param's OUTERMOST suffix (resolving via the incomplete-array path, like allowFlexibleArray) and (b) rewrites the resolved top-level Array to Ptr<element> at BOTH resolution sites (the definitive Pass-1.5 visit that binds the param symbol — so sizeof(param)==8 and body uses see the pointer — and the FnSig param harvest — so call-site checking matches the decayed argument). An inner-dimension `[]` (`int a[4][]`) still fails loud (S_FlexibleArrayInAggregate via the incomplete-element guard), matching C's ill-formedness.

declarations.$prototypeSynthesizesExternComment: c86 (D-CSUBSET-BARE-PROTO-EXTERN-SYNTHESIS, C 6.2.2p5): a file-scope bare prototype with NO in-TU definition (`void sqlite3_free(void*);` — the sqlite3.h splice shape; SQLITE_API expands empty) synthesizes an ExternFunction with NO library binding. The LK11 merge resolves it against a sibling TU's definition (import stripped, calls direct); a bare re-declaration of a SHIPPED descriptor name re-binds to the descriptor's library (goal-2 suppression recorded the map); a survivor with neither fails LOUD at link (K_SymbolUndefined naming the symbol — ld's behavior). `static`/weak protos never synthesize (internal/weak linkage must not bind a sibling TU's public symbol) — they keep the loud H0009. RED-ON-DISABLE: drop this flag -> the shell.c 2-TU probe regresses to H0009 'Ref to unbound symbol' at the first sqlite3_* call.
declarations.$linkageSpecifiersComment: D-CSUBSET-LINKAGE-SPECIFIERS: 'static' -> internal (Local) binding; '__attribute__((weak))' -> Weak; FC4 c1 adds the COMPOSITE key 'visibility:hidden' — linkageFrom forms '<identifier>:<decoded-string-body>' when a specifier identifier is immediately followed by a parenthesized string literal (__attribute__((visibility("hidden")))). 'linkageSpecifierIgnoredRules' skips C23 [[...]] stdAttr subtrees wholesale (D14: parsed, ignored).
declarations.$constexprLinkageComment: FC17 (D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR): C23 6.2.2p3 (N3096) — a FILE-scope object declared `constexpr` has INTERNAL linkage (constexpr joins static in the 6.2.2p3 list). The keyword text maps to {binding:local}; it COMPOSES with an explicit co-present `static` idempotently (both map to local; last-wins is a no-op). This is NOT the noreturn {binding:global}-clobber hazard — that was a no-op default entry overwriting a co-present static's INTERNAL binding; here both entries agree. `extern constexpr` is a parse error in both orders (externSpecifiers admits only the thread-storage twins after `extern` — TLS C1; before that externDecl had no specifier slot at all) — loud, C23-conformant (6.7.1p... extern with constexpr is a constraint violation). RED-ON-DISABLE: drop this entry -> the mir_lowering file-scope-constexpr Local-binding pin goes red.
declarations.$threadLocalLinkageComment: TLS C1 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): both thread-storage spellings map to {threadStorage:true} — the 4th ORTHOGONAL effect axis. A file-scope `thread_local int g;` keeps EXTERNAL linkage (C11 6.2.2 is untouched by 6.7.1p3 — deliberately NO binding entry, so a co-present `static`'s {binding:local} composes without clobber in either order). Pass 1's specifier scan reads threadStorage → SymbolRecord.isThreadLocal; Pass 2 validates.
declarations.$alignasIgnoreComment: C11/C23 6.7.5 (D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS): an `alignasSpec` in a file-scope declaration's specifier prefix (`alignas(32) int g;`) carries NO linkage — skipped wholesale by the linkage scan (like `stdAttr`); the alignment is read by the semantic alignas scan. `attrSpec` is deliberately NOT ignored here (its `__attribute__((weak/visibility))` tokens ARE meaningful linkage). FC16 (D-CSUBSET-NORETURN): the bare `_Noreturn` keyword carries NO linkage → ignored by kind (NoreturnKeyword).
declarations.$noreturnIgnoreNamesComment: FC16 (D-CSUBSET-NORETURN): the GNU `__attribute__((noreturn))` form shares the `attrSpec` rule with the HONORED linkage attributes (`weak`, `visibility`), so attrSpec CANNOT be ignored wholesale here — the `noreturn` IDENTIFIER is skipped by NAME instead (dunder-normalized, so it also covers `__noreturn__`). A no-op `{binding:...}` linkageSpecifiers entry was REJECTED: it would clobber a co-present `static`/`weak` last-wins (an order-dependent silent linkage miscompile). Any OTHER unknown attribute identifier still fails loud H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier. `[[noreturn]]` rides stdAttr (already wholesale-ignored).
declarations.$attrIgnoreNamesComment: FC17 (D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS): the GNU spellings of the SEMANTIC standard attributes — `__attribute__((deprecated[("m")]))` / `((unused))` / `((warn_unused_result))` and the `[[...]]`-vocabulary names when spelled GNU — carry NO linkage; each joins the by-NAME skip (dunder-normalized; a parenthesized string argument is independently skipped by the string-token guard, so `deprecated("msg")` passes whole). WITHOUT these the file-scope GNU spellings hard-fail H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier (probe-confirmed at HEAD). The SEMANTIC meaning is read by the semantic tier's attribute scan (semantics.attributeSemantics), not here. No collision with the honored keys (static/constexpr/weak/visibility:hidden).

kindByChild.$comment: Post-strip children: [topLevelHead, initDeclaratorList, topLevelDeclTail]. childPath [2,0] = the tail's sole child — a `block` node (function definition) or the EndStatement token (variable; whenRule never matches a token). paramsPath/bodyPath are ABSENT: in declarator mode the params live inside the declarator's fnSuffix and the matched block IS the body.

declarations.$implicitReturnZeroForFunctionNamesComme: C99 §5.1.2.2.3: when control reaches the closing '}' of main without an explicit return, the semantics are equivalent to 'return 0;'. The HIR lowering appends a synthetic ReturnStmt(IntLiteral(0)) to main's body if it doesn't structurally terminate. SOURCE-AGNOSTIC: this list lives on the per-language semantic config so each language declares its own entry-fn convention (Pascal's 'program', Rust's 'fn main', etc.) without touching shared HIR substrate. Closes D-LK10-ENTRY-MAIN-IMPLICIT-RETURN.
declarations.$entryFunctionNamesComment: FC5: the program ENTRY-point name(s) — read by the driver's entry-symbol resolution. SEPARATE from implicitReturnZeroForFunctionNames (the C main-style return-0 set); for C both are `main`, but declaring them distinctly de-conflates the two concepts (D-LK10-ENTRY-MAIN-IMPLICIT-RETURN).

declarations.$c25Comment: c25 D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER: the DEFINITION arm of the unified `structSpec`. `definesWhenChild: structBody` makes this row a definition (mint type, bind tag in the Tag namespace, open scope, compose fields) ONLY when a `structBody` child is present (`struct S { … }` / anonymous `struct { … }`); when ABSENT (`struct S v;` / `struct S;`) the node is a pure tag reference, resolved by the paired `structSpec` references-row below (isTagReference). The DEFINE semantics are byte-identical to the former `structSpecifierBody` row — fields now live under the `structBody` child, but Pass-1.5's fieldChildren collection walks the scope this row opens (not direct children), so the extra nesting is transparent. `name: 1` still resolves the tag: structSpec's visible children at definition are [StructKeyword, Identifier, structBody] (tagged) or [StructKeyword, structBody] (anonymous → anonymousNameAllowed synthesizes the name).
c25: the DEFINITION arm of the unified `unionSpec` (mirrors structSpec). `definesWhenChild: unionBody`.

c25: the DEFINITION arm of the unified `enumSpec` (mirrors structSpec). `definesWhenChild: enumBody`. The enumerator lift-to-enclosing-scope behavior is unchanged (fieldChildren.liftToEnclosingScope).
declarations.$fc17Comment: C23 6.7.2.2 (D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE): the OPTIONAL explicit underlying-type clause. When an `enumTypeSpecifier` child is present, the semantic tier resolves its `typeChild` (index 1 = castTypeBase) to an integer TypeKind and uses it as the enum's underlying scalar (scalars[0]) instead of the default I32, AND range-checks each enumerator against it. Mirrors `bitfieldSuffix`'s rule+child-index shape.

declarations.$alignasComment: C11/C23 6.7.5 (D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS): the OPTIONAL leading `alignas(N) …` member-specifier prefix. `head:0`/`declaratorList:1` are POST-STRIP indices — decl_prefix_strip.hpp drops a leading `structMemberDeclSpecifiers` child before positional counting, so the head/declaratorList positions are identical whether or not the member carries alignas. The prefix subtree stays reachable via `specifierPrefixChild` for the semantic alignas scan.

declarations.$threadLocalComment: TLS C1 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): child 0 is now the `externSpecifiers` head wrapper (`extern` + optional thread-storage run) and is this row's specifierPrefix — the shared first-child strip renumbers the role children to [typeRef, Identifier, externTail], so name/type/kindByChild are 1/0/[2,0] (they were 2/1/[3,0] against the raw shape) and stay STABLE whether or not a specifier is present. The linkage scan resolves both thread-storage spellings to {threadStorage:true} (`extern thread_local int e;` — the cross-TU TLS declaration, C11 6.7.1p3) and ignores ExternKeyword by kind (extern-ness IS the rule's identity: nonDefiningDeclaration mints the record; a binding entry would be the noreturn clobber hazard).
declarations.$allowFlexibleArrayComment: c82 (D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-INCOMPLETE-ARRAY, C 6.7p7/6.9.2): an extern OBJECT declaration may carry an INCOMPLETE array type — sqlite3.h's `SQLITE_EXTERN const char sqlite3_version[];`. The absent-length `[]` resolves through the SAME incomplete-array path a FAM uses (interner incompleteArray = the Array kind + a length sentinel, so decay/index consumers keep working while sizeof fails loud). The symbol is non-defining: a sibling-TU DEFINITION completes it at the MIR merge (LK11 name resolution drops the import row); a NEVER-defined one survives as an extern-data import, which the link tier fail-louds on until the extern-data import model lands (the c82 §B, D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT).

references.$c25Comment: c25 D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER: the REFERENCE arm of the unified specifiers. A body-ABSENT `structSpec`/`unionSpec`/`enumSpec` (`struct S v;` / `struct S;`) is a TAG reference — these rows resolve its tag in the Tag namespace, exactly as the former `structTypeRef`/etc. did, via the SAME isTagReference path. The DEFINITION arm (body present) is the paired declarations-row above; the engine routes per-node on the body child's presence (`definesWhenChild`), so a definition is never double-processed as a reference. `nameMatch: lastIdentifier` extracts the tag from [Kw, Identifier]. c35 (D-CSUBSET-FORWARD-STRUCT-DECLARATION): `compositeKind` drives the forward-mint of an INCOMPLETE composite on a tag MISS (an opaque `struct S *` / `typedef struct S S;` whose S is never defined — sqlite3_stmt), so the reference resolves to a sizeable Ptr<incomplete> instead of S_UnknownType; a VALUE/by-value-member/sizeof of it still fails loud (computeLayout incomplete guard). `enum` keeps the fail-loud miss (value-typed).

semantics.$scopesComment: FC4 c1: topLevelDecl replaced funcDefTail — a function's params now live INSIDE its declarator's fnSuffix, so the scope that holds them must dominate BOTH the declarator and the block; the topLevelDecl node is that dominator (harmless for variable-kind topLevelDecls — nothing binds into the scope). c25: the unified `structSpec`/`unionSpec`/`enumSpec` rules are the fieldChildren composites (they REPLACE the retired *SpecifierBody rules). Each opens a scope ONLY in DEFINITION mode (body present) — Pass 1 gates the pushScope on the `definesWhenChild` body-child presence, so a body-absent tag reference (`struct S v;`) opens no scope. The member-body wrapper rules (structBody/unionBody/enumBody) are deliberately NOT scope rules — their fields bind into the enclosing XxxSpec scope.
semantics.$castsComment: FC2: explicit C cast `(T)expr`. typeChild/operandChild are visible-child indices into castExpr = [ '(' , castTypeRef , ')' , castOperand ]. Pass 2 resolves+stamps the type child (typedefs incl. cross-tree-injected ones resolve through the standard type-position resolver), stamps the cast node's RESULT type, and validates the pair against the explicit-cast matrix (scalar<->scalar incl. float<->int, ptr<->ptr, ptr<->int; struct values / void / arrays -> S_InvalidCast).
semantics.$sizeofComment: FC6: sizeof typing. sizeofType = [ SizeofKeyword, '(', castTypeRef, ')' ] → typeChild 2 is the castTypeRef (resolved + stamped through the SAME type resolver casts use). sizeofValue = [ SizeofKeyword, castOperand ] (the operand is typed normally). Both forms are stamped size_t (U64).
semantics.$alignofComment: C11/C23 6.5.3.4: alignof typing. alignofType = [ { alt: AlignofKeyword | AlignofLegacyKeyword }, '(', castTypeRef, ')' ] → typeChild 2 is the castTypeRef (the inline keyword-alt matches ONE token at visible-child 0, '(' is child 1 — SAME layout as sizeofType, so the same index 2). Pass 2 resolves + stamps the type child through the SAME type resolver sizeof/casts use, and stamps the node size_t (U64). TYPE-NAME FORM ONLY — no valueRule (there is no `_Alignof(expr)` form).
semantics.$synthesizedTypesComment: D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY: the standard types the ENGINE synthesizes rather than the source spelling them. C says `sizeof`/`_Alignof` yield `size_t` and a same-pointee `p - q` yields `ptrdiff_t`, and BOTH are ALIASES of a standard NAMED type whose spelling is DATA-MODEL-dependent: `size_t` IS `unsigned long` on LP64 and `unsigned long long` on LLP64; `ptrdiff_t` IS `long` / `long long`. Minting a bare anonymous U64/I64 (what the engine did before type identity was split off representation) produces a THIRD type matching NEITHER named entry — so `_Generic(sizeof(int), unsigned long: 1, unsigned long long: 2, default: 0)` silently selects `default` instead of the arm C requires. Each role maps a data model to a `typeSpecifiers` VOCABULARY NAME; the loader resolves it once (identical machinery to `integerLiteralTyping`), so the engine carries only a resolved (core, tag) pair and never compares a spelling. The REPRESENTATION still comes from the target — `unsigned long` is U64 on LP64 and U32 on LLP64, and the per-model choice of NAME is what keeps size_t 64-bit on both. ILP32 rows are declared for completeness (a declared role must cover every data model, else it would silently fall back to an anonymous core on the uncovered one); an ILP32 format still fails loud at S_UnsupportedDataModel long before these are consulted. Omit the whole block and every consumer keeps its historic anonymous core — a language with no C `size_t` notion (toy / tsql) is unaffected.
semantics.$typeofComment: C23 6.7.2.5 (D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF): typeof typing. `typeRule` = `typeofType` = [ { alt: TypeofKeyword | TypeofUnqualKeyword }, '(', castTypeRef, ')' ] and `valueRule` = `typeofValue` = [ { alt: ... }, '(', expression, ')' ] — `operandChild` 2 is the castTypeRef / expression in BOTH (the inline keyword-alt matches ONE token at visible-child 0, same layout as alignofType/sizeofType). The semantic tier's resolveTypeNodeImpl arm resolves the operand (the type form recurses the SAME type resolver casts/sizeof use; the value form reads the operand's type via subtreeType — UNEVALUATED per 6.7.2.5p2) and, when the leading keyword is `stripQualifiersToken` (`typeof_unqual`), strips top-level qualifiers (only VolatileQual is interned, so strip == stripVolatile; const/restrict are not interned). The result is stamped on the typeof node so HIR's resolveStampedTypeBelow recovers it. CRITICALLY, the typeof operand subtree is made OPAQUE to the coarse volatile/const qualifier scans (subtreeContainsToken / declaratorObjectIsConst) so `typeof_unqual`'s stripped qualifier is never silently re-applied. A bit-field value operand → S_TypeofBitfieldOperand (6.7.2.5 constraint). `stripQualifiersToken` is OPTIONAL — absent ⇒ typeof never strips (a language with only the qualifier-preserving spelling). Absent block ⇒ the language has no typeof surface.
semantics.$bitIntComment: C23 6.2.5/6.7.2 (D-CSUBSET-BITINT): the `_BitInt(N)` bit-precise integer type-specifier. `specRule` = the `bitIntSpecifier` shape = [ BitIntKeyword, '(', assignmentExpr, ')' ] → `widthChild` 2 is the constant-expression whose value is N (const-folded via the SAME constIntExpr sizeof/alignas/static_assert/array-dims use). `unsignedToken`/`signedToken` name the C 6.7.2 signedness keywords the semantic tier scans for among the specifier run's sibling tokens (DEFAULT signed when neither is present). The resolveTypeNodeImpl bitInt arm validates N (non-constant → S_BitIntWidthNotConstant; N≤0 → S_BitIntWidthNotPositive; signed N<2 → S_BitIntSignedWidthTooSmall; N>__BITINT_MAXWIDTH__ → S_BitIntWidthExceedsMax) and interns `bitInt(N, signed)` for ANY valid width — N>64 is a runnable multi-limb type (the C1 N>64 gate S_BitIntWidthAboveC1Limit is RETIRED in C2). Absent block ⇒ the language has no _BitInt surface. Source-agnostic: nothing hardcodes `_BitInt`.
semantics.$alignasComment: C11/C23 6.7.5 (D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS): the `_Alignas`/`alignas` alignment specifier. `specRule` = the `alignasSpec` shape = [ { alt: AlignasKeyword | AlignasLegacyKeyword }, '(', alignasArg, ')' ] → `argChild` 2 is the `alignasArg` (the inline keyword-alt matches ONE token at visible-child 0, '(' is child 1 — SAME layout as alignofType). `typeFormRule` = `alignasTypeName` (the guarded TYPE-form wrapper branch): the semantic tier reads alignasArg's committed reading and discriminates the TYPE form (the committed child's rule == alignasTypeName → alignment = _Alignof(T) via computeLayout(...)->align of the castTypeRef INSIDE the wrapper) from the VALUE form (else → const-evaluate the constant-expression via the SAME constIntExpr sizeof/static_assert/array-dims use). The computed alignment is validated (power-of-two → S_AlignasNotPowerOfTwo; ≤256 → S_AlignasExceedsMax; ≥ the declared type's natural align → S_AlignasWeakerThanNatural; non-constant value → S_AlignasNonConstant; on a typedef/function/parameter/bit-field → S_AlignasInvalidContext) and STORED (SymbolRecord.explicitAlignment for a variable; fed into the composite's fieldAligns for a member). `alignas(0)` is an explicit NO-OP (6.7.5p3) — treated as no override, no error. Absent block ⇒ the language has no alignas surface.
semantics.$packedComment: FC16 (D-CSUBSET-PACKED): C/C23 `__attribute__((packed))` / `[[gnu::packed]]` on a struct/union. `listRule` = the `compositeAttrList` shape (the trailing attribute list after a struct/union body). `attributeNames` = the identifiers that mean `packed` (matched dunder-normalized via the shared stripDunder, so `__packed__` ≡ `packed`; `gnu::packed` matches by its final segment `packed`). The semantic tier scans a structSpec/unionSpec node for a `compositeAttrList` child, extracts each `attrSpec`/`stdAttr` identifier, and marks the composite `packed` (→ the interner's completeComposite → computeLayout removes all inter-field padding + sets natural alignment 1). An unrecognized `__attribute__((X))` identifier fails loud S_UnknownTypeAttribute (typo protection like H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier); an unrecognized `[[…]]` is standard-ignorable (the [[deprecated]] precedent). packed + a bit-field member → S_PackedBitfieldUnsupported (D-CSUBSET-PACKED-BITFIELD-INTERACTION). Absent block ⇒ the language has no composite-attribute surface. Source-agnostic: nothing hardcodes the spelling `packed`.
semantics.$noreturnComment: FC16 (D-CSUBSET-NORETURN): the C11/C23 `noreturn` FUNCTION attribute. `keywordToken` = the `_Noreturn` keyword (C11 6.7.4); `attributeNames` = the recognized attribute identifiers (`[[noreturn]]`/`__attribute__((noreturn))`/`[[gnu::noreturn]]`, dunder-normalized at the scan so `__noreturn__` matches). The semantic tier scans a function declaration's specifier prefix (specifierPrefixNamesNoreturn) for EITHER form and marks the symbol isNoreturn; a DIRECT call to such a function is wrapped `Block{ ExprStmt(call), Unreachable }` at HIR lowering (the wrapIfProvablyInfinite precedent). Source-agnostic: nothing hardcodes the spelling.
semantics.$constexprComment: FC17 (D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR): the C23 6.7.1 `constexpr` OBJECT storage-class specifier. `keywordToken` = the `constexpr` keyword. Pass 1 scans a declaration's specifier prefix (specifierPrefixHasConstexpr, the specifierPrefixNamesNoreturn mirror) and marks each declared symbol isConstexpr (implies isConst); Pass 2's validateConstexprDeclarator then enforces the 6.7.1 constraints AT THE DECLARATION: the initializer must be a compile-time constant (arithmetic scalars fold via the shared CST const-eval engine with floats admitted; a pointer initializer must be a null pointer constant), a missing initializer / a function declarator / a volatile-qualified object type fail loud, and array/struct/union constexpr is a NAMED loud deferral (D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR-AGGREGATE-TYPE). ZERO codegen: a validated constexpr object lowers byte-identically to a const object with a foldable initializer. Source-agnostic: WHICH token is per-language config; the engine never hardcodes the spelling.
semantics.$threadLocalComment: TLS C1 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): the C11/C23 6.7.1p2 forbidden-combination set for the thread-storage specifiers. `register` parses as an inert storage-class specifier in the c-subset (localDeclSpecifiers), so `register thread_local int x;` must reject S_ThreadLocalInvalidCombination rather than silently drop one specifier (6.7.1p2 admits ONLY static/extern beside thread_local; C23 additionally admits auto — the c-subset's auto is the C23 inference specifier and is deliberately NOT listed). `typedef` cannot co-occur grammatically (typedefDecl has no storage-specifier prefix — a loud parse error) and `constexpr` co-presence is detected via the Pass-1 isConstexpr mark, so neither is listed here. WHICH tokens confer thread storage rides the declaration rows' linkageSpecifiers ({"threadStorage": true}) — this block only names the forbidden pairings.
semantics.$predefinedFunctionNamesComment: FC17.5 (D-CSUBSET-FUNC-PREDEFINED-IDENTIFIER): the C99 6.4.2.2 predefined identifier `__func__` (+ the GNU `__FUNCTION__` alias). Pass 1 binds, for EACH spelling, one synthetic symbol into a function DEFINITION's own (param/body) scope BEFORE the params — kind=Variable, isConst (SE4's const check catches `__func__ = x` / `+=` -> S_ConstViolation), type = Array<narrow-string-core, len+1> (the element core is the language's declared string-literal core — the SAME config source string literals type from, never a hardcoded Char). A param named `__func__` collides at its own bind (S_RedeclaredSymbol). HIR lowering FOLDS a read to a string-literal-shaped constant (byte-identical to a real string literal, so rodata materialization / array->pointer decay / `__func__[i]` indexing / sizeof / `&__func__` all ride the existing string-literal paths); identical folded literals share ONE rodata global (the F2 byte-content memo — C99 requires `__func__ == __func__`). ++/--/compound-assign on it -> S_PredefinedIdentifierNotAddressable (no storage slot to write back). Source-agnostic: WHICH spellings are per-language config; the engine never hardcodes `__func__`.
semantics.$attributeSemanticsComment: FC17 (D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS, C23 6.7.13): the standard-attribute semantics TABLE — a closed verb set over per-name rows (all rows structurally identical; ONE unknown-name source of truth). `attrSpecRule`/`stdAttrRule` name the two attribute-specifier shapes (GNU `__attribute__((...))` / C23 `[[...]]`); `bareStatementRule` is the bare attribute-declaration statement (`[[fallthrough]];`). The semantic tier's `scanAttributeSemantics` walks a declaration's specifier prefix (or a bare statement), stops AT each attribute node, extracts each clause's name (last-::-segment, dunder-normalized) + optional string message (the shared decodeAdjacentStringBodies chokepoint), and folds the matched row's EFFECT: `suppressUnused` (the D8 unused-variable warning is suppressed — [[maybe_unused]]/GNU unused), `warnOnUse` (each use-site of the symbol warns S_DeprecatedSymbolUsed — [[deprecated]]), `warnOnDiscard` (a call whose result is discarded as a bare expression-statement warns S_NodiscardResultDiscarded — [[nodiscard]]/GNU warn_unused_result), `none` (KNOWN vocabulary consumed elsewhere or deliberately inert — fallthrough/likely/unlikely/reproducible/unsequenced per C23, noreturn [the FC16 scan owns it], packed [the trailing-composite scan owns it; the leading position fails loud at the HIR linkage tier] — listed so the unknown warning NEVER false-fires on them, design fix F4). An UNMATCHED name in the C23 `[[...]]` form warns S_UnknownAttribute (suppressible — C23 forbids fatal unknown standard attributes); the GNU form keeps its pre-existing loud gates (file-scope H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier; block-scope wholesale-ignore = D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-GNU-BLOCK-SCOPE-UNKNOWN-NAME). Absent block ⇒ the language has no standard-attribute semantics (toy/tsql — the scan never runs). Source-AGNOSTIC: every rule id + name is per-language config; the engine walks the closed effect-verb set only.
semantics.$nodiscardComment: FC17 (D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS): the nodiscard DISCARD-CONTEXT rule ids for the warnOnDiscard effect. A call's result is 'discarded' when the call is the ENTIRE expression of an expression STATEMENT — structurally, the call node's parent is `expressionRule` AND the grandparent is `discardStatementRule` (the TWO-hop check, design-audit fix F1: the call's parent is the materialized `expression` node, so a one-hop parent==exprStmt check would NEVER fire). This shape makes the `(void)f();` discard idiom SUPPRESS by construction (the castExpr interposes → parent≠expression-under-exprStmt) and `x=f()`/`g(f())`/`return f()` correctly no-fire. Comma/paren-wrapped discards (`(f());`, `f(),g();`) are the named deferral D-CSUBSET-NODISCARD-INDIRECT-DISCARD-CONTEXT (warning-only miss). Absent block ⇒ warnOnDiscard rows never fire.
semantics.$variadicComment: FC12a-core (D-FC12A-VARIADIC-CALLEE): variadic-intrinsic typing. vaArgExpr = [ VaArgKeyword, '(', assignmentExpr(apChild 2), ',', castTypeRef(typeChild 4), ')' ] → pass 2 resolves+stamps the castTypeRef through the SAME resolver casts use (so the HIR lowering recovers the read type T) and stamps the node T; it type-checks `ap` is a va_list. vaStartExpr/vaEndExpr = [ kw, '(', assignmentExpr(apChild 2), ... ] → node stamped `void`; ap type-checked va_list; vaStart also flips the enclosing function's uses-va-start attribute so the LIR prologue spills the arg registers.
semantics.$staticAssertComment: C11/C23 6.7.10: the `_Static_assert`/`static_assert` DECLARATION rule. Pass 2 const-evaluates the condition child (visible-child 1 — the assignmentExpr after the keyword+'(') through the SAME evaluator that folds sizeof(T)/enum/arithmetic in an array dimension; ZERO ⇒ S_StaticAssertFailed (message = the OPTIONAL trailing string-literal child), NONZERO ⇒ nothing. The construct lowers to nothing (hirLowering staticAssertDecl → Skip).
semantics.$inlineAsmComment: FC17.9(i) (D-CSUBSET-INLINE-ASM, C23 6.8 / GNU 6.47): the `__asm__` inline-asm STATEMENT rule (asmStmt). Pass 2 (pass2Post) decodes the template child (the stringLiteralExpr, via the SAME decodeAdjacentStringBodies chokepoint staticAssert's message uses) and REQUIRES a strictly-empty decoded string; a non-empty / whitespace-only / malformed-escape template fails loud S_InlineAsmNonEmptyTemplate (real per-target asm text is the D-CSUBSET-INLINE-ASM-TEXT deferral). The empty form lowers to nothing but a MirOpcode::CompilerBarrier fence (hirLowering asmStmt → InlineAsm → CompilerBarrier). Invalid ⇒ the language has no inline-asm surface (toy/tsql — the check never runs).
semantics.$genericComment: FC16 (D-CSUBSET-GENERIC-SELECTION): C11/C23 6.5.1.1 generic selection. `rule` = the genericExpr shape; `controlChild` = the visible-child index of the controlling `assignmentExpr` (child 2 — after the GenericKeyword + '('). `assocRule`/`typedAssocRule`/`defaultAssocRule` name the association shapes so Pass 2 discriminates each `_Generic` child: a genericTypedAssoc's FIRST child is a castTypeRef (resolved+stamped through the SAME type resolver casts/sizeof/va_arg use — a VALUE in type position fails loud S_UnknownType) and its LAST is the result expression; a genericDefaultAssoc is the `default:` fallback (no type). Pass 2 lvalue-converts the controlling type (strips top-level volatile, the isAssignable precedent), matches it against each typed assoc's resolved type for COMPATIBILITY (interned TypeId equality via sameType), enforces EXACTLY ONE typed match (S_GenericSelectionAmbiguous on >1) OR the default (S_GenericSelectionNoMatch on none-and-no-default), stamps the genericExpr node with the winner's RESULT type, and records the winning association's result-expression node (nodeToSelectedExpr side-table). The dedicated HIR lowering (hirLowering.genericRule) lowers ONLY that recorded sub-expression.
semantics.$compoundLiteralsComment: FC3.5 sweep-c3 (D-CSUBSET-COMPOUND-LITERAL-TYPEDEF): the compound-literal type position resolves + stamps through the SAME type-position resolver casts use (typedefs included). typeChild is the visible-child index into compoundLiteralExpr = [ '(' , castTypeRef , ')' , braceInitList ]. NO operandChild / cast-matrix validation — a compound literal is C 6.5.2.5 postfix syntax, not a conversion.
semantics.$builtinFunctionsComment: c103 (D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-UMULH): compiler intrinsics exposed as builtin functions (SE6 builtins scope, shadow-able by user decls). `__umulh` = the high 64 bits of the u64*u64 128-bit product (the MSVC/sqlite intrinsic used by sqlite3Multiply128/160 on _WIN64). `lowering:umulh` binds the call to a DEDICATED MIR op (x86 `mul r/m64` -> RDX, arm64 `umulh`) rather than an ordinary call/import -- a compiler intrinsic is not a linkable symbol. Available on every target that declares the umulh mnemonic vocabulary (x86_64 + arm64); a target lacking it fails loud at lowering.

builtinFunctions.$comment: C99 _Complex (D-CSUBSET-COMPLEX §7.3): the always-injected complex builtins the <complex.h> macros route to. The `signature` form spells the `complex<f64>` result/param the scalar params/result axis cannot express (the _InterlockedCompareExchange ptr<> precedent; parseType decodes `complex<..>` via the M1 codec). `__builtin_complex(re, im)` constructs a complex from two reals (by-address materialized — the FIRST aggregate-returning builtin, CRITICAL-2). creal/cimag extract the real/imag F64 component; conj negates imag. Cycle-1 F64-monomorph (D-CSUBSET-COMPLEX-MONOMORPH-F64).
builtinFunctions.name: __builtin_complex

semantics.$builtinStdbitComment: FC17.9(b) C23 <stdbit.h> (D-FULLC-STDBIT): the 56 `__builtin_stdc_<op>_<T>` builtins = the 14 N3096 §7.18 bit operations × 4 concrete widths {uc→U8, us→U16, ui→U32, ull→U64}. Each op's 4 width rows SHARE one `lowering` tag (stdc_leading_zeros … stdc_bit_ceil) → 14 BuiltinLowering enumerators → 14 hir_to_mir arms that COMPOSE the 3 hardware primitives (Popcount/Clz/Ctz) + universal ALU verbs (ZExt/Sub/Add/And/Or/Not/Shl/ICmp/Mul) into the exact width-correct, single-eval, branchless formula (NO new MIR op — pure composition over the proven bit-count substrate). The arm reads the operand's EXACT width W∈{8,16,32,64} from its param core (guaranteed by the pre-BuiltinCall arg coercion) → zero-extends to the promotion width P∈{32,64}. RESULT cores per C23: U32 for the 11 count/index/width ops; Bool for has_single_bit; the OPERAND core (U8/U16/U32/U64) for bit_floor/bit_ceil (they return the argument's type). These are the include-gated substrate for shippedLibs/stdbit.json's `stdc_*` macros (56 concrete + 14 per-format `ul` + 14 4-way `_Generic`); always-injected like `__umulh` (reserved `__builtin_` namespace, shadow-able). ★ MANDATORY shift-UB guard (bit_floor/bit_ceil): every `Shl(1, amt)` has amt∈[0,W-1] on EVERY branchless branch (bit_floor amt=bitWidth−(x≠0); bit_ceil amt=bitWidth(x−1)&(W−1) + the ==W overflow→0 select) — never an unguarded shift.
semantics.$builtinBitCountComment: FC17.9(b) (D-CSUBSET-BITCOUNT-INTRINSICS): the 3 hardware bit-count primitives as 6 GCC-compatible builtins (the walking skeleton for C23 <stdbit.h>; the 14 stdc_* ops layer on top). Each returns `int` (I32, the GCC signature) and takes an unsigned 32-bit (`__builtin_popcount`/`_clz`/`_ctz`) or 64-bit (`…ll`) value. `lowering` binds each call to a dedicated pure-unary MIR op (Popcount/Clz/Ctz) — like `__umulh`, a compiler intrinsic, not a linkable symbol; lowered to a native instruction (x86 POPCNT/LZCNT/TZCNT, arm64 CLZ + RBIT-then-CLZ for ctz) where the target declares the mnemonic, else a branchless SWAR bit-trick sequence (arm64 has no scalar GPR popcount). ★ DSS DEFINES clz/ctz at 0 = the width (LZCNT/CLZ/TZCNT hardware semantics — count = 32 or 64 for a zero input), NOT GCC's UB-at-0: a SAFE SUPERSET (every input GCC defines returns the same value; the 0 input GCC leaves UB is given the natural hardware answer).
semantics.$builtinAtomicCasComment: c104 (D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-ATOMIC-CAS): `_InterlockedCompareExchange(LONG volatile* dest, LONG exch, LONG comp) -> LONG` -- the MSVC atomic compare-and-swap intrinsic (returns the ORIGINAL *dest; stores exch iff original==comp). Win32 LONG = i32 (LLP64) -> the 32-bit CAS. The UNDERSCORE name is the real intrinsic exactly as the Windows SDK spells it; windows.json's `InterlockedCompareExchange` macro expands to it (the winnt.h convention), so sqlite's `#if defined(InterlockedCompareExchange)` takes the direct-intrinsic path and its calls land here. `signature` (full type-text, c104) instead of scalar params: the dest param is a POINTER. Lowers to x86 `lock cmpxchg` (implicit-RAX comparand/old) / an arm64 LDAXR-STLXR acquire-release loop.
semantics.$builtinAtomicExplicitComment: FC17.9(d) atomic cycle-1 (D-CSUBSET-ATOMIC): `atomic_load_explicit(volatile A* obj, memory_order) -> C` and `atomic_store_explicit(volatile A* obj, C desired, memory_order) -> void` — the C11/C23 <stdatomic.h> §7.17.4/§7.17.7 explicit-order scalar accessors. Like `_InterlockedCompareExchange` these are always-injected compiler intrinsics (a non-linkable op, SE6-shadow-able) rather than shipped symbols; they route to the SAME MIR AtomicLoad/AtomicStore ops a bare `_Atomic` access uses, so the Phase C per-order LIR fence matrix realizes them. Monomorphized to `int` (i32) this cycle (defer atomic_long/llong/ptr → D-CSUBSET-ATOMIC-MONOMORPH-I32). The trailing `memory_order` arg (load arg[1] / store arg[2]) is CONST-FOLDED at hir_to_mir into the AtomicLoad/AtomicStore `payload` (0..5) and DROPPED from the operand list (AtomicLoad=[ptr], AtomicStore=[value,ptr]); a NON-constant order falls back to seq_cst (5) — over-fencing is C11-legal. The user-facing surface (atomic_int, memory_order_*) is include-gated in shippedLibs/stdatomic.json.
semantics.$builtinTypesComment: FC3 c1: the TEXT-keyed type-name table (typedef-style positions + the load-time fallback for single-word ladder/promotion names). The KEYWORD grammar path resolves through semantics.typeSpecifiers below (the C 6.7.2 multiset table) — both tables declare the SAME widths, and `long` carries the LLP64 override on BOTH so the two paths can never disagree (D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH).
semantics.$typeSpecifiersComment: FC3 c1: every VALID C type-specifier multiset (C 6.7.2 6) over the shipped keyword surface; combinations are ORDER-FREE (the engine canonicalizes by sorting token kinds; `long long` = LongKeyword twice). Invalid combos (`unsigned float`, `short long`) reject by ABSENCE -> S_InvalidTypeSpecifierCombination. `long`-bearing rows carry the LLP64 (Windows) 32-bit override; `long long` stays 64-bit everywhere. FC17.9(e) (D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE): the `long double` row carries the per-format coreByLongDoubleFormat axis — base F64 for f64-axis formats (pe64, macho-arm64: long double has double's REPRESENTATION), F80 (x87 80-bit) for x87-80 (elf/macho x86_64), F128 (binary128) for ieee128 (elf-arm64); an axis-less format (wasm/spirv) leaves the row UNREALIZED -> S_LongDoubleFormatUndeclared. D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY: this table is the LANGUAGE (vocabulary) axis; `core`/`coreByDataModel`/`coreByLongDoubleFormat` are the TARGET (representation) axis, and the two are INDEPENDENT — `name` is what identity interns on, so `long` stays distinct from `int` under LLP64 and `long double` distinct from `double` on the f64 axis (both previously COLLAPSED, making `_Generic` ambiguous and `int* p; long* q = p;` silently legal). `rank` is the C 6.3.1.1 conversion rank of the NAME (long long 4 > long 3 > int; long double 3 > double > float), used ONLY to break a usual-arithmetic-conversions tie between two operands of the SAME core. Named ONLY where a collision is possible: int/short/unsigned int/unsigned short/float/double/bool/void/plain char stay UNNAMED so they remain the ANONYMOUS representative of their core (integer promotion and enum-underlying synthesis re-mint anonymous primitives of those kinds; naming `int` would make a promoted `char+char` stop matching a declared `int`). Every row sharing a name must declare the SAME representation on every axis (loader-enforced, C_InvalidSemantics).

typeSpecifiers.$comment: C99 _Complex (D-CSUBSET-COMPLEX §6.2.5): the `complex` row flag wraps the resolved ELEMENT float core in interner.complex() at the specifier-resolve site (semantic_analyzer.cpp), so `double _Complex`→complex(F64), `float _Complex`→complex(F32). `_Complex int`/`_Imaginary` (no row) reject via the absent-multiset discipline (S_InvalidTypeSpecifierCombination), the `unsigned float` precedent. `long double _Complex` reuses the LD axis: its element is F80/F128 on x87-80/ieee128 (arith walls loud there), F64 on the f64-axis formats (decl/layout/sizeof/ABI-reject work).

semantics.$literalTypesComment: FC3 c1: IntLiteral's I32 is the BASE core; the integerLiteralTyping ladder below refines the width by magnitude/suffix/radix per C 6.4.4.1. FloatLiteral's F64 is the BASE core; the floatLiteralTyping suffix map below refines it per C 6.4.4.2 (FC3.5 sweep-c2 / D-CSUBSET-F32-CODEGEN closure: `1.5f` now types F32 — the F32 arithmetic surface shipped, so the typing flip pairs with real codegen). true/false are C23 keyword literals: type Bool with config-declared FIXED values (1/0) — the engine never decodes their text as a number.
semantics.$charStringLiteralComment: C 6.4.4.4: a character constant has type `int` (so sizeof('c')==4, NOT 1) — CharLiteral→I32. C 6.4.5: a string literal has type char[N+1] — StringLiteral declares stringArray:true with element core Char, so the engine builds Array<Char, decodedLen+1> per occurrence (the HIR value path independently lowers the same Array<Char,N+1> via the shared char_decode). charConvertsToArith (below) admits the C char<->int implicit conversion in BOTH directions: int->char (`char x='c';`) and char->int widening (`int y=c;`; codegen materializes the Char->int SExt). Pinned by CharParamReturnedAsIntIsClean + the char_value corpus.
semantics.$intCrossSignednessComment: C 6.3.1.3/6.5.16.1 (D-CSUBSET-INT-CROSS-SIGNEDNESS-CONVERT): admit the signed<->unsigned implicit assignment conversion (`int x=u;`, `u=i;`, `return i;`/`f(u)` across the signedness boundary) — value-preserving in range, modular out of range; needed for full-C + SQLite's int/unsigned/size_t mixing. coerce()'s arithmetic-core arm materializes the width-exact Cast. SAME-signedness narrowing (`int x=aLong`) is the SIBLING gate intSameSignednessNarrows below.
semantics.$intSameSignNarrowComment: C 6.3.1.3/6.5.16.1 (D-CSUBSET-INT-SAME-SIGN-NARROW): admit the same-signedness integer NARROWING implicit conversion (`short s=anInt;`, `signed char c=anInt;`, `int i=aLong;` across init/assign/call-arg/return) — value-preserving in range, truncating (modular) out of range; needed for SQLite, which narrows everywhere. The isAssignable rank arms admit rank(rhs)>rank(lhs) only when true; WIDENING stays unconditional. coerce()'s arithmetic-core arm materializes the width-exact Cast (MIR Trunc) — the SAME path cross-signedness narrowing already uses, so NO codegen change. Together with intCrossSignednessConverts this completes the C integer-conversion matrix.
semantics.$intFloatConvertComment: C 6.3.1.4/6.3.1.5/6.5.16.1 (D-CSUBSET-INT-FLOAT-CONVERSION): admit the int<->float implicit assignment conversion in BOTH directions, gated independently. intConvertsToFloat -> an integer flows into a float lhs (`double d=5;`, `f(anInt)` to a `double` param; the sqlite kahanBabuskaNeumaierStep(pSum,iBig) shape feeds an i64 to a `volatile double`). floatConvertsToInt -> a float flows into an integer lhs (`int n=aDouble;`, truncating toward zero). coerce()'s arithmetic-core arm materializes the MIR SIToFP/UIToFP (int->float) / FPToSI/FPToUI (float->int). Flipping either to false -> that direction errors S0003 S_TypeMismatch (the int_float_convert corpus is RED-ON-DISABLE). Completes the C arithmetic-conversion matrix alongside intCrossSignednessConverts + intSameSignednessNarrows.
semantics.$floatLiteralTypingComment: FC3.5 sweep-c2: C 6.4.4.2 — a floating constant's type is keyed by its SUFFIX alone (unsuffixed -> double, f/F -> float, l/L -> long double). Type names resolve through typeSpecifiers/builtinTypes at LOAD time like the integer ladder's candidates (the `long double` row copies the coreByLongDoubleFormat axis map, so `20.0L` types per-format: F64/F80/F128). The loader cross-checks every numberStyle.floatSuffixes spelling is covered exactly once + an unsuffixed rule exists. FC17.9(e): l/L are in BOTH suffix sets — the tokenizer's per-shape suffix rule keeps integer `20L` an IntLiteral (integer suffixes first on an integer-shaped token) while `20.0L`/`2e1L` take the float suffix.
semantics.$integerLiteralTypingComment: FC3 c1: C 6.4.4.1 EXACTLY — the FIRST candidate (per suffix-group x radix-class) whose range holds the decoded magnitude is the literal's type. `decimal` = no declared numberStyle prefix matched; `nondecimal` (hex/octal/binary) adds the unsigned candidates per the C table. Type names resolve through typeSpecifiers/builtinTypes at LOAD time, so LLP64's 32-bit `long` falls out of the data-model select (2147483648 is `long` on LP64 but `long long` on LLP64). Suffix GROUPS list the exact numberStyle.integerSuffixes spellings they cover; the loader cross-checks every declared suffix is covered exactly once. A magnitude beyond the last candidate -> S_IntegerLiteralTooLarge.
semantics.$arithmeticConversionsComment: FC3 c1: C 6.3.1.8 usual arithmetic conversions, declared (the engine algorithm in analysis/semantic/type_rules.hpp is closed-verb config-driven; languages WITHOUT this block keep the legacy commonType path). integerPromotion: operands below `int`'s rank promote to int; `alsoPromote` pulls char/bool (outside the width-rank lattice) into promotion WITHOUT the engine hardcoding C's view of char. mixedSignedness `rank-prefer-unsigned`: unsigned rank >= signed rank -> the unsigned type, else the (strictly wider) signed type. promoteComparisons: comparisons convert operands to the common type (so -1 > 0ul compares as U64 -> true) and still yield Bool. shiftResult `promotedLeft` (C 6.5.7): a shift's type is its PROMOTED LEFT operand (the right operand never contributes, so `i32 << i64` is I32) -- the engine reads this verb instead of hardcoding C's special shift rule; the alternative `commonType` types `<<`/`>>` like an ordinary binary op (common type of both).

ruleMappings.$comment: FC17.5 (D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE): ZERO HIR changes — lowerVarLikeInto reads the declared type off the SYMBOL (rec->type, which Pass 1.5's inference arm wrote), never re-derives it from a head, so the headless rule lowers through the same VarDecl row as varDecl.

FC17.9(i) (D-CSUBSET-INLINE-ASM): the empty-template `__asm__ [volatile] ("")` lowers to a core InlineAsm leaf → MirOpcode::CompilerBarrier (HIR→MIR). UNCONDITIONAL: the empty-vs-non-empty gate lives in the semantic tier (semantics.inlineAsmRule → S_InlineAsmNonEmptyTemplate), which aborts before codegen on a non-empty template, so a rejected asm's MIR is never emitted — clean separation, mirroring staticAssertDecl (semantic gates the condition; the lowering row is unconditional).

FC17 (D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-STATEMENT): a bare attribute-declaration statement (`[[fallthrough]];`) lowers to NOTHING — the emptyStmt Skip precedent, zero C++. The enclosing `declOrAttrStmt` wrapper is deliberately UNMAPPED: the unmapped-statement soleMeaningfulChild PassThrough peels it (for BOTH branches — a varDecl statement reaches its VarDecl row through the same peel).

hirLowering.$stringLiteralPrefixesComment: C11/C23 6.4.5 string-literal openers → element core. The narrow StringStart row is auto-seeded by the loader (elementCore=Char) but listed here for clarity. WideStringStart (wchar_t) declares its FORMAT-keyed width AS CONFIG DATA via elementCoreByFormat (elf/macho→I32 the POSIX width, pe→U16 the Windows UTF-16 code unit — D-FFI-STDDEF-WCHAR-PE-WIDTH, the SAME per-format axis stddef.json's wchar_t typedef declares via when.format); elementCore=I32 is the default/fallback for a format-agnostic caller. No engine tier branches on format — the semantic tier resolves the row via a pure config-map lookup (LiteralPrefixEntry::resolveElementCore, mirroring builtinTypes' coreByDataModel) and the HIR tier reads the resolved element core back off the stamped node type. All five openers share the coalesced StringLiteral body token.
hirLowering.$charLiteralPrefixesComment: C11/C23 6.4.4.4 character-constant openers → SCALAR element core (the C23 per-prefix TYPE rule — a char constant's type depends on its prefix). The narrow CharStart row is auto-seeded by the loader (elementCore=I32, the C `int`) but listed here for clarity; the semantic tier keeps it in the flat literalTypeIds int path (other integer-literal consumers depend on it) and OVERRIDES only the wide rows. WideCharStart (wchar_t) declares its FORMAT-keyed width AS CONFIG DATA via the SAME elementCoreByFormat axis the wide-string L" row uses (elf/macho→I32 the POSIX width, pe→U16 the Windows UTF-16 unit — D-FFI-STDDEF-WCHAR-PE-WIDTH); elementCore=I32 is the format-agnostic default. No engine tier branches on format — the semantic tier resolves via a pure resolveElementCore lookup and the HIR tier reads the resolved core back off the stamped body token. All five openers share the coalesced CharLiteral body token. Unlike a string (an Array), a char constant is a SCALAR value (one code unit) typed by this core.
